Warm, rigorous class environment; available outside of class; great feedback on assignments; provides ample networking opportunities. Not a class for slackers.
Heavy readings and discussions, unclear and even misleading guides for assignments, non-transparent grading criteria, the worst course I took in this school
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Had to drop after class #1. Never listed the text on the bookstore site and told us the textbook a week before classes started. She's clueless how to use Canvas yet treats her Tech Support very poorly. Gave unclear instructions on assignments and offers no clarification. Group assignments due the second week of class but she never assigned groups.
A true dolt.
Avoid at all costs!

This was the absolute worst professor that I have ever had. He digresses constantly and no one could grasp what he was talking about. I would avoid him at all costs. I had him for Judicial Pol and Logic of Collective choice. The logic class was the worst, he does not use a text book and the TA's didnt know anything.
This professor was horrible and his TA's were incompetent. He brags that his class will be hard and gives no one an A.
He is not the best teacher but I really dont like.

Prof.  Robertson is experienced and inspiring to students.  I, personally, dislike rating in terms of grading....this is preposterous.  Unfortunately, American students are hyper-focused on grades, not learning.  However, Dr. Robertson is a generous teacher, and impressive advocate.  His style of teaching is warm and receptive.

Professor Robertson is so passionate about what he teaches. He is genuinely involved and cares about the students. He is also super nice and helpful but especially very knowledge. I loved learning from him.

Dr. Robertson is one of the most amazing professors I've ever met. He is an incredibly intelligent person and he is really willing to share his knowledge to all students. I personally think the statement that he is unapproachable makes no sense- he's actually easy to reach out. Very thoughtful and open-minded person once you get to know him.

The grading criteria for assignments was unclear at times but our TA would always clarify. He had a habit of inviting people to ask questions and then saying that those questions should be asked privately/after class, but that could be because it was an online course. Overall, it was an okay class.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Professor Robertson is the real deal. He's an amazing professor with a wealth of knowledge. His classes definitely have a good amount of work, but you will learn a lot. I appreciate the space he creates for other opinions, and I also appreciate the push back he gives because I think it creates better social workers. Work hard you'll do well.
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Get ready to read--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: A great professor. A lot of people might think him to be arrogant. That's probably because he likes challenge students in terms of their existing beliefs and thoughts. However, I think he is open-minded and respect people with different thoughts and backgrounds. I especially appreciate that. The requirements for paper is also clear and specific.

The Bad: a bit arrogant (but nowhere near the terrible Marian Riedel). The Good: I actually learned a lot in this class, which is more than I can say for many of the classes at this school. While he can be aloof, I think John Robertson is also a pretty good teacher and I appreciated his class making me work and challenging my beliefs.

Lectures are good. You have to do weekly assignments, but they're not a big deal. He can come across as a little arrogant, but I'm always glad to hear from a professor who will share their (well-supported) opinion.
Very Clear. Likes to challenge students to think from a different perspective, which I found really helpful and inspirational. Helpful. Has a sense of humor.
Definitely someone who needs to learn people skills first.  Does not respect students or their input.

Professor Robertson is an extremely intelligent, knowledgeable and effective teacher. He may not seem approachable in class, but if you get to know him, he's a very empathic and thoughtful person. I learned a lot from him about welfare policy and about myself.

Terrible professor. Avoid him if you can. He puts no effort into class, and so the two hours we spend there each week are useless. VERY confusing on assignments, and on grading as well. Worst of all, he is extremely pretentious, and very disrespectful to his students. I love the topic, and am really disappointed about this class.

Prof. Robertson is incredibly intelligent and I loved his lectures because I felt like I was really learning a lot. I can see what others are saying about the non-personability, but that didn't really bother me. I would recommend him because I did learn a lot however it does seem to take a lot to impress him.
He actually is approachable  You just have to approach him.  He is a very kind.  Once you approach him he'll be very helpful.

Knowledgeable alright, but I've had knowledgeable profs who are actually also personable. This prof is probably the one I dislike the most. He can't engage the class, people hardly participate, because he hasn't managed to create a good class atmosphere. He does not know how to reflect, respond, and relate to students' feedback. Preachy in lecture.
Very knowledgeable but very unapproachable and unclear about expectations for assignments.

I found Robertson to be very knowledgable but not at all personable. Whenever I had a question about an assignment, he made me feel like an idiot and refused to provide clarification. While I learned a lot in his class, I found him to be completely unapproachable. If policy isn't your cup of tea, I wouldn't suggest taking his class.

Prof assigns a LOT of reading and expects you to remember them in minute detail for his ridiculous pop quizzes (also the only policy prof who additionally quizzes you on the ny times). He is okay as a person, just wish he would be cognizant of everything else most SW students deal with and didn't set unrealistic expectations for reading.
one of the best professors in policy related classes
No Comments
No Comments
Class is interesting, and he adds real life through use of stories from the NYT.
Uses provably inaccurate information in course materials, exclusively pushes his personal agenda, lectures based on ideology and bigotry instead of historical fact.
Not accommodating, does not follow syllabus, goes off on rants about his personal beliefs, not a good class environment.
Unaccommodating and unreasonable professor.
Amazing teacher so nice
Did not learn a thing
Great professor. Very helpful and let's those who disagree with him state their opinion.
Excellent professor, skilled at engaging students in meaningful discussion, enriches discussion with well-founded insight and an impressive commitment to teaching
incredibly intelligent, and very knowledgeable and insightful.  Highly recommended.

Excellent professor by all standards. He has his opinions and isnt afaid to share them- and DOES allow others express different opinions. I think he is an excellent- esp. when he argues with facts and data rather than sheer emotional passions
Interesting narrative. Open to differing opinions. Engaging.
Brilliant, kind professor.  Fair and let's students speak
Inspirational and very humorous professor, take his calss once you'll know what the ratings are about.
Dr Kuras is brilliant. His lessons are fascinating, thought-provoking, inspiring and very profound. Immensely grateful for his classes.
a life-changing class. So inspirational. these courses he teaches are gifts.

Dr Kuras is a great professor. He makes you think, reflect upon your assumptions/bias and question everything you are taught. His explanations are very clear. Class topics are deep and engaging. 1000% recommended
Fascinating professor. Compelling and inspiring lectures.
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Graded by few things--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment:
Her leadership requires students to open up their mind and show weakness. Yet, it is a bit hard to do these when you don't feel genuine emotions from your instructor who doesn't seem to care that much. 

I do think she did a good job breaking down key leadership concepts and had profound experience. It is just she was a compelling leader for me.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--So many papers

reviews.comment: Kirsti did an amazing job to guide us in our capstone project and provided valuable knowledge and feedback throughout the semester. We had to put in the work, but with Kirstis support paid off with a solid published report. I think any course with her as a lecturer would be interesting, especially since she has a wealth of knowledge/experience.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Participation matters--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS

reviews.comment: I found Kirsti to be an excellent Capstone advisor: she is critical, but her criticism is constructive, specific and never arbitrary. Unlike many professors, she took the time to review our work product and give us helpful feedback as well as help us decide on a clear strategy for the research and analysis portion of the work.

Kirsti provided valuable guidance as an advisor to our team. I was disappointed that I didn't get the grade I wanted as I put in a lot of work. That aside, she was very thoughtful in handling the team, taking the time for healthy team dynamics and getting to know us. She skillfully balanced being an expert and giving us control of the project.
The professor supported us in writing a great capstone project that was published. Hard work. long hours.

Capstones are intensive,but Dr. Samuels made the project manageable. She has a wealth of knowledge and practical experience that she was willing and open to sharing. She took time to learn each of her students. She played up our strengths and assisted us in navigating through our challenges. Great teacher; Seasoned professional; Highly recommended.
Kirsti seemed disengaged and unavailable and provided little to no guidance to the team during the project. While she has an interesting CV, I did not feel like I learnt anything from her.

Kirsti was a great instructor. Her feedback on work was thoughtful and grounded in her wealth of experience in both peacebuilding and leadership development. She knew each student well, and was conscious of our group dynamics. (Note the bad reviews come from folks who declined to provide their grade. You get out what you put in.)

Kirsti is a wonderful professor and leader. She is very intentional, giving strategic guidance when needed, but also giving teams space to figure things out. Our team had an emergency abroad, and Kirsti went above and beyond to give us the tools to deal with the situation, provide support & show compassion. I would recommend her class to everyone.

Kirsti is a seasoned peace-building professional. She is knowledgeable of the theory and the practicalities of the field, which makes her a great tutor for a graduate course. I can only imagine that a course with her would be not only interesting but intellectually challenging.
She is super nice and knows her field!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: I liked Professor Kostina and her class. It gave a good understanding of how Russian media has changed over the last thirty years since the USSR ended. There was a lot to cover, but her approach was organized and balanced. The class included weekly reading responses, a mid-term paper, and a final paper.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Gives good feedback--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Prof Kostina is kind, supportive, and well-organized. The lectures and discussions are structured and engaging. Watching Russian documentaries and movies was a great way to experience a culture I hadn't encountered before. She grades fairly and provides detailed feedback on the first draft of the final paper.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: A wonderful professor who is friendly, approachable, and flexible. She strikes a great balance between lectures and class discussions.Classroom environment is low-pressure and allows for different ideas and perspectives. Her structured and organized approach, along with the variety of readings and media genres, is a huge plus.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: Great class, enjoyed the readings and the lectures. The professor is super nice and approachable. Very knowledgable about literature, the immigration, politics, culture, theater, music, ballet, you name it - she can tell you tons about it! Just be open minded and ready for the discussions (hint - do your HW, read the assigned books!)
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Inspirational--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Loved her! Very passionate about the topic and goes above and beyond to help the students and make it accessible! Highly recommended.
Highly recommended! An easy A, if you are ready to read, write weekly reading responses, and participate in class discussions. One class presentation and a final paper. Graded generously and fairly.

Prof. Katsnelson is one of my favorites! Her passion for the subject and deep knowledge about the books and authors are truly impressive. I especially loved how she brought the material to life with visits to émigré artists' galleries, which added such a unique perspective. She's both brilliant and kind, and I enjoyed every moment of her class!

I missed course evals this summer, so I had to find a way to make it known what a wonderful professor she was. Truly one of my favorite professors to date, she cares so much and her classes are so engaging. Her class was a real highlight in a hard time, and she is one of the sweetest people Ive ever met. And shes clearly quite brilliant!

As an international student, it was tough getting used to grad school, but her class helped me feel confident I could follow the material. She clearly puts effort into making topics accessible for everyone. She has a way of explaining things that just lands. The collaborative environment she created in class was also a highlight for me.

Took her class last semester and liked it so much I signed up again. The case studies and hands-on exercises make ethics feel practical, not just theoretical. Prof Scherling gives thoughtful feedback and genuinely cares about students. This course made me think seriously about what I want to do in tech policy. Highly recommend.

This class has been instrumental in my professional development, and I still reference a good amount of the material now that I work in the tech sphere. Professor Scherling always made time for flexible office hours for any student who wanted them and provided carefully crafted, constructive feedback on assignments.

Worst professor at Columbia.
Acts as if he knows the topic, folds at any real question, poses as a good coder and just regurgitates online info.
Would probably fail or cheat on his own course.
Would not recommend.
Does not teach well
Run away from professors having a claw shape in the rating curve and woot woot, such a huge claw!
I agree with all the other reviewers
Does not give feedback on where to improve as a competitive programmer
Don't know who made this guy a professor
Would not recommend
Professor is known for emotional manipulation and the course is bad
Heard from seniors that he blames students for cheating in the last week of semester and the grade is effected
Well nah as the other reviewers say

Tough class, but Unstuckstudy was a lifesaver for study prep. Lectures are hit-or-miss, so rely on the free textbook and stay engaged. Overall, a challenging course, but manageable with the right strategy.
Who made this guy a professor
just no why this
just nah hard skip
fr no cap this course be trash
straight away be no taking this course
Would rather not take his classes
He brushes off people who are not able to answer very rudely
Attended a few of his classes and decided to drop
Thinking to take the course this semester but after reading these reviews I am going to drop it now
I hear from reddit that this professor asked his students to rate him well... that's why he has so many positive reviews from 1 year back and a lot of negative reviews after that
for people considering taking his course, I highly recommend you to drop the idea and take some other course
terrible. acts as if he knows what he's talking about but just recites from online resources. worst professor i ever had.
Just truly awful
I walked out of the class after the first day of class
His mandatory problem sets are useless
Tonnes of better courses at Columbia
Literally orders his students to rate him well
Rather not take
Don't take his courses
Easily the worst professor
grades unfairly and poorly
worst professor forever
One of the worst there can be. Very few students in the class. Don't take
Complete garbage

Absolutely horrible professor. Super self-centered and constantly talks about himself and his "20 hours a day" grindset. You can learn more through online resources and this class was an absolutely waste of time.
Genuinely think the man had a m
I ended up dropping out after the second class.

Literally the worse, I'm baffled at how he has a high score. Cannot teach, grades incredibly unfairly, gives no opportunity for help despite incredibly hard material, flat out rude, and pretty much just expects you to know everything yourself despite his label as a "professor". You are better off using Google for free.
His teaching methodology is garbage. Wouldn't recommend taking any of his classes

We had a thoroughly unpleasant experience. I would not recommend this to anyone. I do not recommend enrolling in this course. Frankly, you could achieve more by joining Columbia's club. Why spend thousands of dollars when you can access the same benefits for free?
does not give good grade
Bad Professor, worst attitude, does not want good for the students. Take it at your own risk.
Would never recommend!
I do not recommend
ok, to be honest, I wouldn't repeat this mistake of taking any of his classes

One of the worst classes in all of Columbia! You get a bad grade plus learn absolutely nothing out of it. I have taken more than 12 classes in Columbia and took this course because I heard he gives good grades. But that was a lie! Do competitive programming by yourself guys.
No feedback provided about any mock interviews in the class. The major reason to take the class was to get some feedback for the interviews. But there was none at all. What a waste of money!
I agree with all the recent reviews, he is genuinely the worst
Took both Competitive programming and Tech interview prep this semester. Learnt absolutely nothing out of it. Christian does not even have a phd and he literally cannot teach.

Teaches absolutely zero useful content. Uses his own product/startup to try to teach the course, which is filled with bugs that cause students to waste hours on homework (is this even allowed?). Spends half the class talking about himself and his work experience.
better classes available to take in Columbia
don't take
Attendance isn't mandatory, at times there was only the TA who came to the class. Better use your money elsewhere
Took the class last year and I found it to be really bad
Check the course contents before taking this course. Attend a few classes then decide.
don't take it
Not worth
Lectures are useless. Please don't take this class
Inspirational. Legend in coding.
Very bad classes. Will not wish the class on my worst enemy,

He loves emotional control. For those who are interested in his course, please be prudent. The pressure and trauma are way too far worth what this course can give. He will accuse students of cheating without any proof. Please think twice before taking this course.
Threaten students. Chaotic instructions. Unhelpful materials.

Ill be the middle ground and say in the earlier version of this class grades were reflective of the sum of a semester of work and a lot were happy to have found a low-stress CS course. More recently tho, I would agree with the newer reviews. The lectures were not relevant, it's disorganized and communications from the prof are off putting.
I did not go to any single class and received an A, what's the point of this class?

Learned nothing. Rubric is a mess. Expectations unclear. Accuses students of cheating with no evidence during finals week (from reading the comments, seems like I am not the only one). Wouldn't wish this class on my worst enemy.

Please note the reason there are many good reviews they are "extra credit".

This class is horrible. The lectures are disorganized and useless. Rubric unclear and changes all the time. Threatens students during finals w/ "cheating" accusations, which are straight-up untrue. Threatens student  they are "failing" the class, also untrue.
Really passionate about lectures and contests. Great prof!
It's easy to get an A in this course. The professor is friendly and reachable. He also cares about students' success.
Great semester, thank you Professor for all you did for u$$$!

The professor knows his material very well and is very excited to teach it. It can be challenging at times but the professor provides ample opportunities to gain points. He is very generous with his time outside of class and seems very invested in his students. Highly recommended!
Professor Lim is a very empathetic professor that really strives to make sure people can improve their problem-solving abilities.
Unclear expectations, crazy difficult course content.

Amazing professor who is kind and approachable and is very eager to help students out! If you are looking to improve problems solving skills and prepare to build confidence and skills to get a job take this class. Easy to get a good grade if you are diligent about building your points!
Genuinely cares about his students! Tesla Info session event was awesome too
He knows his stuff and provides solid lectures that get the job done. He is professional, organized, and available to help when needed.

Professor Lim really cares about his students success. Every class he advertises his mentoring opportunities, whether it be career, grade, CP abilities. I highly recommend  Professor Lim, particularly if you are looking for a career in tech or quant, and even to improve your problem solving skills!

[COMS4995: Competitive Programming]
Lectures are well organized and suited best for students wanting to level up quickly in competitive programming. The professor is also incredibly enthusiastic and helpful. However, this course is not for beginners and you will find the material extremely challenging if you're looking to just prep for interviews.

Prof Lim gives me a strong "resume-builder" vibe. He asks his students to upload their resumes and rate him on all public platforms in exchange for "assignment points." He also initiates linkedin connections. I think this is not a morally right action. Students are here to learn. Students are not here to better the reputation of the professor.
I cannot learn anything from this course

Professor Lim is kind, but his course is an absolute train wreck. Students are thrust into contests with zero guidance whatsoever. The lectures are useless: he spends most of the time covering "logistics". When he actually teaches, he covers obscure graduate-level topics nobody understands, instead of the fundamentals to get started.

Professor Lim is one of the most caring, helpful professors I've ever had. He knows the subject matter incredibly well and is invested in the success of the students. He gives personalized advice and recommendations and great feedback. I feel so lucky I took one of his courses and can not say enough good things about him.
Amazing professor

You'll be happily remembering course(s) with Yongwhan as you reminisce about university for years to come.  His combination of high levels of empathy, support, personality and intelligence are extremely rare in academia.  People are quickly realizing his targeted approach for tech interview prep delivers the best ROI during their time as a student.
He is so nice on providing lots of resources and supporting outside class!
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Yongwhan cares more about his students than any other professor I have had at Columbia. He is incredibly knowledgeable and enthusiastic about the material and it is frankly inspiring as a student. He is also willing to hold office hours at absurd times during the day (3am - 6am) so students can meet with him even during his busy schedule.

Professor Lim knows his algorithms pretty well and have a lot of industry insights. Plus, he cares a lot about students in his course and actually listens to the feedbacks he receives and acts on them.
Yongwhan Lim is a professional and easygoing professor. I learned a lot from his competitive programming course.

Professor Lim has great passion for competitive programming and really care about students. He provides 1v1 mock interview session and gives resume suggestions outside of the class for all students. The most supportive and helpful professor I've ever met. Also, met my best friend in this class and teamed up together to prepare icpc practices.

Yongwhan is amazing!!! This class is so fun and stress free while being very helpful for preparing for the technical interview process. I really learned a lot of invaluable skills and enjoyed my time in this class.
One of the most important classes I have taken.  If you really out in the time and effort - it will be time-consuming but you will learn so much!

One of the must take courses at Columbia, will learn a lot and definitely use a lot of the skills you learn. Overall, Professor is very approachable and tries his best to make the course an amazing experience for each and every student in the class
Very well-organized course. Great lecture material, a lot of office hours opportunities.

Create professor, you can really feel his excitement about competitive programming and his commitment to helping students. You definitely get out of the course what you put in. It's pretty easy to get a good grade by just coasting along but if you put in the time and effort you will absolutely improve in CP/interviews and gain a great mentor!

The course content may be hard for those who didn't have CP experience in the past, however, the overall course is totally doable if you are willing to devote time to it. The instructor is really helpful and accessible.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: There's a lot of work, but also a lot of optional extra credit.
The most responsive professor I've ever met! Full of knowledge in the field of technical interview.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Excellent course. Professor really cares for your success
I love this Professor Lim's 4995, it encourages me to practice algorithm everyday for my interview!

If you're looking to work on your technical interview or cp skills, this is the guy to talk to. One of the best professors at Columbia. No doubt he cares about you and wants you to succeed. The class requires attendance and hardwork, but he'll help you through it. Super friendly and funny too!

An incredible professor who is very smart and accomplished. Columbia University is truly lucky to have a professor like him who brought an amazing class to this university. Something that was much needed to prepare students for full-time roles and interviews.

I took Prof. Lim's Competitive Programming course in Fall 23 with absolutely zero competitive programming experience and essentially no DSA experience outside of Data Structures.
The learning curve was/is absolutely enormous, but you will learn a lot and get to solve a lot of interesting problems.
Great professor, clear and well-prepared lectures, very caring and involved.
If you have any intent to apply to tech jobs, do not hesitate to take either of Professor Lim's courses. He has great connections, teaches exactly what you need to know, and cares greatly about you.

Took competitive programming with Prof Lim this semester and he's literally so kind and extremely knowledgeable and just wants to see his students succeed. Probably the best Professor I've had in my time at Columbia.
Professor Yongwhan is a very very nice guy. He possesses excellent knowledge of competitive programming. He is always happy to meet with his students, and this is very helpful.

Professor Lim is the most helpful professor I've encountered at Columbia. He really cares about the success of his students and is very approachable outside of class for everything related to CS career development. I really enjoyed his class and would highly recommend it to anyone who's looking to get better in interviews for tech.
Prof. Lim really cares about students' performances and feedback in the class.

Professor Lim made a computer science class that was actually useful to a career in tech. From a well thought out grading policy to plenty of coding interview tips, he turned 50 wannabe FAANG interns into problem solving machines.
The professor is so encouraging and very nice to all students!
supportive and approachable lecture. The course is beneficial to those who want to get a tech job.

If your goal is to get a FAANG offer, I strongly recommend you to take this course because I did receive an offer from FAANG while taking this course. This course will push you to attend many programming contests every week. You will learn to solve technical interview problems skillfully, and professor Lim is resourceful and very accessible.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: Yongwhan can work out all the difficult coding problems and get you an easy understanding. After this course, I can ace nearly all the medium problems on leetcode and some of the hard ones.

Amazing professor. He is one of the top competitive programmers in the world and so willing to share his knowledge with others. And Professor Lim also gets so excited by the material; his energy is contagious.
One of the most approachable, friendliest, and smartest professors I have ever met.
Really knowledgable and cares a lot about students!
Great professor who truly cares about their students!

A class that respects your time as a student while still teaching you as much or even more than others? One that teaches you productively and actually teaches you relevant things for your career without the debilitating stress of exams? Now that's sigma.

Professor Lim is caring, open minded and puts in the work. 10/10 would recommend!

Professor Lim is very approachable and genuinely cares about his students. He was constantly trying to improve the course to better accommodate his students. I would recommend taking this class for interview preparation and career advice, it is one of the most useful classes that will actually help you succeed outside of school.

A great professor that really wants to see you succeed! While the course requirement seems daunting he really tries his best to see you succeed in the course and is very approachable about any issues you may be facing.

The professor is very knowledgeable in DS/Algo as he comes from a strong competitive programming background. He clearly understands the dynamics of how companies hire, on what skillsets, etc. He makes sure to cater well to the students' interview preparation!
Overall, I recommend this course to anyone interested in landing their dream job as SWE.

One of the best lecturers I have ever met.
Very passionate and friendly, and provided helpful career advice!
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Yongwhan Lim is awsome!
Professor Lim is one of the best instructors I have ever met! So caring and smart. I love this class!!!

Course is well structured. It includes basic algo pattern and mock interview, helping students to learn both coding and soft skills. Professor Lim is extremly warm and generous. He is very easy to reach outside class and is always willing to help.
Very supportive and nice professor!
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: AMAZING PROFESSOR, like seriously one of the most kind, helpful and knowledgeable teacher's I've had on a college level.
As long as you're trying in the course professor Lim is willing to work with you and support you in any way necessary. He wants you to succeed both professionally and academically
Professor Lim is very kind and supportive!! I love his lectures.

Professor is really helpful and accessible outside of class and helped me a ton! Extremely smart and good at what he does so his lectures are great too. There are a lot of opps to engage with other students as well as the prof during lectures.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Great professor!!!
Really kind and caring. Genuinely passionate about his students' success.
The most helpful professor I met at Columbia.

If you are preparing yourself for a job hunt or programming contest, Yongwhan is your guy.  He helped me do a mock interview and correct my resume out of his own time during spring break.  He is also super reachable at any time.  The course lay a good foundation for tech job interviews.

He's extremely knowledgeable in competitive programming, resume screening and tech interviews in general, and is very invested in the success of his students. This course will make sure that you participate in coding contests regularly, and there'll be ample amounts of office hours later to go over solutions and general tactics to get better.

This was hands down my most intensive, but also favorite course here at CSSW. The information I learned as a future ACP clinician was so relevant and I feel like Professor Pirro's connections to the LCSW work made things more understandable. Her class feels scary and overwhelming, but if you put in effort to learn, you will pass. She's the best!

To uncritically go through the DSM is bold in a social work school. Prof Pirro is "old school," which here does not equate to wisdom, but rather an unwillingness to hear other povs, use of outdated language ("transgenderism," anyone?), and only grades based on massive tests with unclear directions. Take for the LMSW test if you must, but take PF
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: The best course I have taken at CSSW. She is a dedicated clinical professor with very good acting skills, which helped me understand all disorders better. She wrote beautiful songs for our graduation. She gave us love.

Prof. P's passion, dedication, & compassion for the nuance & depth of the humanness of disorders, is inspiring & life-changing. She cares deeply for her students & their learning. Lots of reading; cumulative tests (3) w/ freebie q's; GREAT for non-psych majors (tests help prep for the LMSW). One of the BEST professors I have taken thus far at CSSW!

Prof Pirro is a caring, passionate person, but I did not love having her for Psychopathology. She's old-school and doesn't use a PROP lens. Felt like typical psychology class rather than social work class. Her syllabus is ridiculous, but I didn't do most readings and I was fine. Just learn the slides, don't buy the books. Get Pascale if you can.

Don't buy all of her books before the first class, as she will go over ways you can consolidate.  That being said, you will read a LOT of material.  She is incredibly engaging, cares about each student, and balances lecturing with examples from her own career.  A must for clinical students.

Prof. Pirro is one of the best teachers I have ever had. She expects a great deal  and gives a great deal. While the readings are extensive, you leave this class REALLY knowing something. If you are committed to be a clinical social worker this class is a must.

15 MATERIALS FOR CLASS, of which 7 are text books. She uses ALL OF THEM! There are questions on the exam from all books, you can't get away with not ready any of them. The exams are cumulative and really hard. She has way too much to say. AVOID!
Absolutely amazing experience! Professor Lindeman is helpful, kind, and knowledgeable. He is a fair grader. We had a lot of discussions in t

Never take this professor. He posted the grading policy only at the end of the semester and appeared to assign grades arbitrarily. I was shocked that Columbia could have such a poor instructor (nearly everyone felt the same). His class was a complete waste of time.

Look at the reviews from his previous position at Syracuse University.
Amazing professor! He is well-read, smart, and very funny. Take him!
From this post down, “John Jay” shows up twice, and there are three back-to-back posts all on 12/06/2023. Looks like the guy made the account himself.

As a first-year student at John Jay, he was an amazing professor to have! He truly cares for his students' writing and gives great feedback. Be prepared to get the best feedback. He helped me understand the way I write, and I would definitely have him again!
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: Professor Lindeman is truly one of a kind. His passion for teaching makes every class engaging and easy to follow, even when the topic is challenging. Professor genuinely care about their students, always taking the time to answer questions and provide helpful feedback. Thanks to his clear guidance and encouragement. Highly recommend his class!
Professor Lindeman is wonderful. He has great writing tips for papers and is clearly organized in thought and on paper. As a first-year student at John Jay, he made the experience smooth an easy.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: As a first year student at John Jay, I'm so thankful to have such an amazing professor. He take's class time to go over all the material and goes around if any students have questions about absolutely anything!!!! Once he get's to know his students he's extremely down to earth and very understandable about class work.I'd recommend to everyone.

AMAZING!! Professor. Lindeman is an amazing teacher who's very understanding and easygoing!!!! He takes the time to go over each assignment carefully, making sure that the students understand what they're doing. If you submit an assignment late, he won't penalize you but will help you get back on the right track.

Overall, for my first course, this professor took the time to understand his students as well as give a great understanding of all his work. He takes the time to explain all the work given and provides class time for all students to begin and ask questions if needed. He's very easy to talk to and is very understandable.

Professor Lindeman is caring, funny, and knowledgeable. You will need to read the articles which are super interesting. Had total of 3 take-home essays and 3 inclass essays. If you follow his feedback, you will get an A.

Prof. Lindeman is the best ALP instructors I've ever met at Columbia. He gives a very crystal-clear guideline for essays and good at explaining how to write well-organized and profound essays. Also, he doesn't give a lot of assignments as other instructors do. I can tell you without any hesitation that he is the best ALP teacher at Columbia.

Professor Hanlon demonstrates strong professionalism and a clear, structured teaching style. Course content closely reflects ARE exam material, which I found highly effective for preparation. She goes beyond standard instruction to ensure understanding, and I felt well prepared. I recommend her approach for students pursuing licensure.
Nice Professor
Great ADVANCED Class! I believe I'm finally on the right path to completing my goal of passing these exams.
I had her back in 2011 for ARE 4.0 and she contributed to me passing these exams with their syllabus back then.

as this is a crash course for test prep, professor Hanlon is very concise in the material that is needing to be delivered to achieve success. Professor Hanlon is very knowledgeable in all subject matter which has been a great advantage to having her as our instructor.

After 12 weeks, this course has been invaluable. The instructor is deeply knowledgeable about the exam content, keeping sessions focused without the fluff other courses add. She answers every question, takes student feedback seriously, and genuinely wants her students to pass. Highly recommend to anyone preparing for the ARE!

I love the online syllabus. It is the best product from the class. The professor was very knowledgeable. I would have liked it if the lectures were preferred before course and it has a structure to it.

Professor Hanlon's ARE prep course provided a lot of valuable information that is nearly impossible to find elsewhere. The pace of the course was too fast making it difficult to fully understand some of the more complex concepts during class. The syllabus is extremely helpful, but I found myself having to figure out concepts on my own after class.

The ARE's are brutal. Take it from someone who has used both Amberbook and Black Spectacles before - the Gradium syllabus goes far above anything those courses will give you and ensure you will be as prepared as possible for the ARE's. Umber was fantastic and you can really see the passion she has to share her knowledge and resources.

Extremely knowledgeable and an incredible teacher.  Would recommend her to anyone getting ready to take the ARE Exam. Knows the material and has a way of teaching that prepares her students by teaching specific topics and strategies for taking the exams.

WOW!!! The depth of knowledge is huge. Prof. Hanlon was clear and to the point. The amount of information the ARE requires us to know and memorize is vast and wide, but the prof. always brought it down to a human scale.  She was available for office hour's to answer all my questions. I would take this course again. Its a lot of info.. so buckle-up.
The class distills a great amount of difficult information into discrete and comprehendible materials and explains them a concise, educational fashion. Very good class.

She was a thorough and attentive instructor who clearly explained the material and took the time to answer student questions. I also appreciated that she held regular office hours during the week for anyone who needed extra help or clarification.

Professor Hanlon is awesome, very thorough and has expert grasp of every content area. She happily answers questions and will review a difficult subject if you need. I would happily repeat the course just to reinforce what I've learned

I would HIGHLY recommend Professor Hanlon for the online Gradiom ARE prep course. I have learned more in these sessions than I have using any other study material. She really knows her stuff and is very intentional in the syllabus content. I wish I could have taken her course sooner.
Very knowledgeable in the subject matter. Organized, easy to contact when taking the course, always open to feedback, efficient learning. I enjoyed the class!
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: I really enjoyed taking this course. Professor Hanlon is not only proficient in the knowledge needed, but her live explanations and active syllabus' are the best I have come across for the ARE. I also appreciate the ability to extend the syllabus once the lectures end to continue studying if failed an exam.
So much to learn regarding the ARE.  I thought I had studied everything until I took the ARE PPD course!  I learned new things in a very fast and efficient manner.

I took the gradium/funkaar course for the ARE 5.0, and could not recommend it enough for trying to pass the ARE. I passed 3 tests before this class and got stuck. The materials and knowledge that were provide to me were beyond exceptional and I am very grateful for this class.

Very knowledgeable professor that leads you directly toward success with mastery of the material. Her advanced course is fast paced but highly focused on exactly what is needed to conquer exams with confidence.

Umber Hanlon's full course at Funkaar Institute was challenging but informative. The pace felt too fast at times, and the syllabus could be more refined. Extending the course length would improve it. Still, I gained useful insights and preparation from the experience.

Very knowledgeable but almost to a fault. Do not expect an intro level or basic course. Get ready to buckle down and take tons of notes fast. She readily answers questions if you can keep up with the advanced material fast enough to formulate one. Highly suggest going to office hours where she will reiterate material a bit slower.

Professor Hanlon is detailed in her teaching and ensures complex systems and topics are explained clearly and understandably. Although the class moves quickly, the material remains manageable and comprehensive given the time constraints and subject matter.

As an older student in this class, who has 30 years working in this field, I say that she has more knowledge than I do! Not only does she keep her knowledge honed fresh and relevant, she also knows how things actually work in the real world, not just theoretically for passing tests. I will take her classes again.

The professor is always willing to answer questions and clarify concepts. She explains the material clearly and in a very structured way. I learned a lot in this seminar, and it really helped me understand which areas I need to focus on and how to prepare for the exam more effectively.
its good but difficult for second language people to understand
She is an expert in this.
its a little bit hard foe second language people
Professor Hanlon is extremely knowledgeable about all of the architectural exams and makes learning a large amount of information both easier and more engaging.

Professor Hanlon's motivated for students to pass the ARE and CSE and tries for a comprehensive set of information on the needed knowledge. There are times when some clarity's been sacrificed to get through all the material—but construction code is, sadly, messy to start with so it would be hard to clarify any further than the original muddy laws.

Professor is extremely knowledgeable in the course material and breaks it down in a way that is easy to understand. She is great at explaining core concepts while also is able to answer very detailed questions on the subject matters. Great Professor.

Professor Hanlon is incredibly intelligent, and it shows in the programs she creates and the answers she gives. The class moves at an extremely rapid pace (which I much prefer). She can come up with examples on the spot to support the learning environment and has answered every question thrown her way in depth and accurately. Among the best.
Have taken Professor Hanlon a few times now, great teacher. Extremely knowledgable.

I joined Professor Hanna's class three times, and it was always excellent. Her clear explanations and engaging style made tough topics easier to understand. I highly recommend her Funkaar sessions to anyone preparing for the ARE exam. Truly worth your time and effort!
great work

Professor used every second to flood you with all the possible knowledge you will need for these exams. Wasn't sure what to expect signing up for these classes & extremely happy I took the course. I know it will help me finally pass these tests & every penny I paid was worth it. The perfect professor for the lectures & very helpful with questions.
Knows the material

This course requires daily homework assignments. Ensure that you have set aside time and offered yourself grace as topics can be heavy on some days and lighter on others. Overall Professor Hanlon is very knowledgeable and has done the prep work necessary to guide the course and provide a professional foundation.
Professor is very knowledgable and is great at explaining difficult topics.
Professor Hanlon is very knowledgeable of the material and can clear answer question and concern, I would take courses with her again
Amazing content, great lecture, great professor. I understand less people take the course if it's longer but it would be great to have the option. The syllabus is very heavy and can get overwhelming.

She works quickly but slows down to clarify and explain if you do not understand something. She is friendly and very knowledgeable. Some of her math-based explanations are a bit complicated but understandable after some reviewing. Overall great professor; very pro-student.

She was very knowledgeable about the content she was sharing with the class. She gave the class extra examples and always provide clear feedback on question. She was clear and concise on the information she was teach in each lecture.
Extremely knowledgeable on the subject coursework
You will learn a lot.

Great preparation for the ARE and covered the material quickly and efficiently.  At the end of the day, I feel like I was provided all of the tools, calculations and vocabulary to successfully pass the ARE.
Too much focus on some items and not enough on others

Professor Hanlon is very knowledgeable with lots of years of experience.
Extremely clear and knowledgeable about ARE. Her directions, inspirational ,and practical manor in class might have altered my career in the most positive way.

Professor Hanlon is a fantastic teacher. The course she taught is very dense with information and it is easy to get overwhelmed. However, she always encouraged the students and reminded us that we could get through the course and exams. The digital syllabus provided does not compare with anything I've seen before.

She has the knowledge and passion for the breadth of ARE topics. I am grateful for the excellence in which she instructs with to help us prepare for the exams. She has been inspirational to me continuing in the field in which I wanted to give up.

Professor Hanlon's has a direct approach to teaching and explaining the information. The topics are current and relevant for exam prep, she's very positive and provides clear and concise explaining the topics. The syllabus is helpful but your participation does matter and expect lots of note taking. Worth the time.
A highly knowledgeable instructor who breaks down the topic and explains it in a way that everyone can easily understand the concept

This online ARE prep course was very tough, but proffessor Hanlon is very knowledgeable and gives excelent consolidated information, lectures and tips to be able to focus on exam content material.
I highly recommend this course it is a great refresher course for anybody that has been out of Architecture school for a while

The professor is very knowledgeable of the subject criteria and helps through various modules of the program. It is very good to get the tips and tricks of the exam so we don't get into the pitfalls of the exam so that we can have a better grade outcome. This course also provided knowledge that will help me even beyond the exam.

it's difficult to study for the architecture exams as it exists, but the course was easy to follow and loved how she broke down complex concepts into easy to digest bits of information. a wonderful professor and always has the student's best interest in mind.

The lecture is well-delivered, with valuable insights across all extended topics. The exam strategies provided are practical and effective. That said, the class duration and dense content can make it challenging to maintain focus throughout, suggesting that pacing or content distribution could be optimized for better engagement.

There is often miscellaneous information given, however a rhythm of lecture that allows your brain to grab an understanding of the important concepts.  Be prepared to participate in the beginning of each class session.  The animated content helps explain concepts of multi-faceted systems.

clear and concise conceptual lessons, could be a little more thorough explaining relationships between systems and WHY things are the way they are and the application of the concept. Also I struggled to follow the math explanations and the relationship between formulas and how to apply them, and why that formula applied to that specific scenario.
It is a lot to learn in short amount of time. This professor navigates the syllabus well to cover many topics. Graphics and animations help make information easy to absorb.

Among the best professors I've had. Moves very quickly to cover a wide span of information and topics. She is extremely intelligent and can answer/figure out every question. The information provided and how it is explained is clearer than any other professor I've taken. Keeps the class on topic when we start to stray too far. Highly recommended.
Professor Hanlon was able to explain difficult concepts in a clear, concise manner.   I found her course extremely helpful to solidify my understanding of many areas covered on the ARE.
Professor Hanlon makes the difficult topics easy to understand.  She is the best professor that I ever had.

Professor Hanlon does a great job helping student focus their study efforts for the ARE exams which are such a deep bench of content.  It was helpful to understand concepts that would allow you to through questions on the exam. It was a natural progression, though an aggressive timeline.
Clear, concise and pertinent information

Professor Hanlon is very clear and knowledgeable. She's extremely professional, knows what she's talking about and gives very focused information. She's passionate about teaching students and it really shows. I couldn't have gone through the ARE without her guidance
Would definitely recommend Professor Hanlon. Super clear and concise. Teaches you exactly what you need to know and how to do it effectively under exam pressure.
This is a fast-paced course with a lot to cover in a short time. Professor Hanlon is knowledgeable and really helpful, and the material is good for studying for the exams.
Professor Hanlon's instruction is great, its a load of information but it definitely helps you when taking the ARE exams.
very Knowledgeable and helpful!
Class schedules need to be longer.  So, much information to learn and cover in a short period of time.  Study materials are current with testing.
Very knowledgeable and informative. Provides clear and consolidated course information and is a great resource regarding the exams!

I was very please with Professor Hanlon knowledge, patience and willingness to keep us on our toes during the long online classes. The material covered was very useful. It was a fast passed curse, but she guided us very well.

Professor Hanlon was a great asset to my studies and preparation for the PA, PPD & PDD exams.  Having been away from school and working fulltime.  I would suggest to anyone seeking to pass these extensive exams to give the program a try!

Professor Hanlon utilizes code to explain why items are required, and how to acheive the required outcomes. She will do deep-dives to explain the full concepts, and points out how these relate or don't relate to similar items.

Professor is inspirational and helping nature, she explains better I got my all answers and solved my doubts…. I want to thanking you funkaar institute for giveing me this professor for teaching me….and thank you professor Hanlon for teaching me….

Very knowledgeable on content and methodical in answering questions. I enjoyed her classes.

I'd take more of her classes.
Great use of lecture tools for graphic display notes.  Showed much concern for success of students.  Discussed pertinent information for exam prep.
Class and Materials are perfect, but they are too compressed for these heavy materials that need more time.

Professor Hanlon is an expert in her field and an inspiration. She is very clear, commands the room and explains concept in a way that is easy for everyone to understand. This course is very tough and I think she does a great job. Would highly reccomend.

VERY GOOD, IS QUICK SOMETIMES- SO PAY ATTENTION.

The professor speaks clear and notes are helpful.  Assigning homework questions that are reviewed the next session would be nice.  Clear homework for each class would keep study straightforward.

VERY dense course material and compressed into very little time. Professor explains subjects clearly and is open to questions or clarifications throughout course. VERY recommended course&professor.

she expects diligence and hard work & also inspires it out of you!

i highly recommend her!

Professor Hanlon is very knowledgeable about the exam prep material, and I geniunely trust her expertise and experience level with architecture, structure, MEP and other related trades.

she knows and explains well the are content.

Professor Umber has such a outstanding way of teaching and presenting the information so that you have the confidence to pass all of the Exams.  Passed all the exams in less than a year! Thank You!

fast pace and go thru a lot of materials, so I 'd suggest you study a little bit before attending the class.

Construction documents, schematic design, etc

very knowledgeable about the subject matter.

Excellent!

Professor Kazmi is A MUST for passing ARE Exams, extreme knowledge, very motivational, to the point.
I took her vignette courses and with her help i passed all i took with little effort. She is good.
Umber is excellent at preparing candidates for the ARE.

ARE fast Course was the kick start I needed, I know how and what to study. Have passed 3 tests in 2 months and waiting to hear on the 4th. The class is very fast moving, so be prepared to wite fast! She'll make u pass the exam, she met my expectation and went beyond. worth investing tuition, definitely.
Umber is very helpful and extremely knowledgeable about test content.  She has helped me pass 4 exams in the last 2 years!!
Great presentation.

She is an excellent instructor and really knows her stuff.
Too fast

Great teacher for ARE class.  Concise and to the point with the information.
Excellent Professor. Have recommended the class to many others.

Very knowledagble on the subject, makes you feel at ease.

Amber knows the material inside and out. The review is extremely intense but hopefully will end up serving me right. Thanks.
Very helpful in explaining the concepts
Fantastic! Understands what we were looking for from class and gave us more than we expected.
It was a very useful class, and one of the best one.  I was hopping that building systems and construction systems can break into a two days class with more detail info.
Alot of stuff to cover-The structures class was intense and fast pace- but simplified to its bare bones of concept- hope it works like the 4 other exams that I passed from taking the Funkaar classes-
Extremely helpful in explaining the concepts
She was very knowledgeable about the subjects and gave very helpful tips for studying for the ARE

Jacksonville AIA 2009 seminar.   I took 4 ARE exams(PPP,SD,SPD,BD) over the Christmas holidays and followed Umber / Funkaar?s study guideline/methodology and I just the got back the results and I passed all of them. WOW!

I took the Jacksonville AIA 2009 seminar. Umber moves at an intense pace but the information is clear and specific. Take notes and try to get into and understand the frame of mind needed for sitting the ARE. Umber is awesome- trust her wisdom and guidance 100%  I took 4 ARE exams(PPP,SD,SPD,BD) over the Christmas holidays and followed Umber / Funk

I took your ARE Bootcamp course in Jacksonville back in November, specifically the prep courses for SPD and PPP.  I'm pleased to say, I took both exams the following weekend and I just received notice that I passed both exams.  Thanks for all the help, I'm certain that your course was instrumental in my success.
am proud to report that on Dec. 24 I received my final pass letter on the ARE. Passed all divisions the first time and I really owe it to you and your class.
Umber handles all the material very well given the fast pace of the course.  I would recommend this course to others who are looking to get through the ARE as fast as possible.

Umber Kazmi is very confident and she inspires the same confidence. She knows the subject and the class is well prepared. She made me think these exams are not impossible to pass. Please, use a microphone. Rising your voice should not be an option base on # of hours. It was a pleasure attending to your ARE 4.0 seminar. Rodolfo Martin

I thoroughly enjoy Prof. Kazmi's teaching & content. You do have to be on your toes in order to keep up, but there is a world of content to cover in just a few hours. I would recommend adding 1 more hour to cover the vignettes because we went through them so fast it was hard to take notes, and even the smallest mistake could get you lost.

To much content to cover in the time period alotted. If more time is needed than schedule the class longer. Don't skip over things because it was covered in a previous class some students don't take all seven classes!!!

I took the ready fast courses for Lateral Forces and General Structures.  I passed the exams about one month later.  I am certain that this course was an essential part of my preparation in order to succeed on the first attempt.

Took the BT test prep just prior to my exam at the end of June.  Umber covered all of the issues on the exam and her way of presenting fundamentals is superb.  Overhead projector that breaks the problem down into a series of simple steps is very helpful.

Umber is very "to-the-point".  Every minute of the 4 hour Building Technology course was utilized with helpful information.  She is very knowledgeable on the ARE and I am so glad I drove up from San Diego to take this course.  8 weeks later and I just received my pass letter in the mail.  Thanks!!!

This rating is for the ARE Architecture Exam. Umber Kazmi offers the most concise and pinpoint exam information available. Her system truly gets you organized and gets the job done. You will pass the Architecture professional Exam.

Excellent outline and way of manipulating the formulas.  Very helpfull when asking questions.  The pace she outlines is brutal, but probably would be ok for students three years out of school.  I would highly recommend this course and I wouldn't have passed without it.
I passed 6 of the tests ive found out about so far, prof umber's class plus a strict study sched is the best way to pass the ARE in one solid attempt.
Umber shares great tips to help you work with the ARE graphic computer program! She makes good use of the time allotted by focusing on what is required to pass the exam.
Great instructor.  Could not have passed without her class. I took classes with her for LEED, ARE and CSE.
I really enjoyed the class. The content was very thorough and informative. Umber didn't was any time. I recommend the course to anyone study for the ARE.

She is very helpful and clear in her teaching; the only complaint is that sometimes it just feel like the class ia  bit rushed because there are so much information to cover, in a short amount of time.  I would like to see the class expand to cover the materials even further in depth.
Class was clear & concise.  Especially useful were specifictips on passing Leed 2.2.  I signed up at last minuete & have no regrets.
Strick, direct, clear.

I for one had a terrific experience with these courses. I passed all four which I took on the first try. I think Umber did a fabulous job teaching each division I took. It helped prepare me for the actual exams and I had a better understanding of material which helped build my confidence for each of those exams.  I highly suggest these courses be
Class covers material well, but very fast paced.  Would appreciate a little more explanation given in Building tech. handout to cover at home.  Overall techniques better than ARE solutions.

On 6/16/2009, I received my final passing grade for ARE 3.0.  The Funkaar seminars helped by taking the fear out of the test.  I was well prepared for whatever was thrown my way.  What a great feeling.  I passed all exams in less than one year.  Thank you.
I don't know how anyone could pass the ARE without taking this class!
Clear and consise presentation of difficut material.  I would highly recommend this class for anyone taking the ARE.  Save your money on the books and flashcards and take this class instead.
Taking the funkaar course w/ umber proved to be a very usueful. She was clear in explainig the materials and answering to many of the confusing NCARB un-knowns! I'd highly recommend her.
It was very helpful.Thank you very much! :)
Good class overall, but difficult to follow so much information in such a fast past setting
Very open and knowledgeable professor. I'm still waiting for the results of my exams, but what I've learned in her class is critical and will serve me well for the rest of my career.
Clear concise and knows her material backwards. Willing to engage new thoughts and honest when she doesnt know Encouraging
thank you.
very well taught and excellent use of the time alotted for a complex exam
Very focused and provides you with the tools and organization needed to approach the ARE test study, especially ME and GS.

I took the fast course seminar in Puerto Rico for MMs, CD and PD- 3.1. So far had passed the 3 graphics. Studied prior to your seminar for MM and for 2 weeks studied your suggested topics and chapters and PASSED. Thanks. The best review for the ARE so far. I couldn't have done it without your seminar.
EXTREMELY KNOWLEDGABLE AND CARING TOWARD HER STUDENTS
Enjoyed the class.
Excellent instructional approach - extremely practical and license-focused.
I passed all m written tests for my architecture record exams.
I could not imagine passing the exams without the professional help and expertise that Umber pointed out.Thank You for all your help.
Very focused on the task at hand.
I enjoyed prof. Kazmis class because she satyed focus on the important aspects required for the test.
She is a great teacher and it was a very helpful class for reviewing and prepping for the ARE's. She covered an amazing amount of information in a short time frame.
Great Teacher!
GREAT PROFESSOR
I like it. Her explanation is great enough for me to understand the subject. She answered questions sincerely during classes.
I have had Umber for 4 or 5 classes now and she is always very helpful & willing to explain concepts until understood. She packs alot of content in, as well.
eXCELLENT LESSON PLANNING
great class. A lot to take in but a great use of your time
Extremely knowledgible. Very helpful.

Professor Kazmi is one of the best professors I've had at NSAD.  She's professional, intellectual, well-organized and driven in her presentations and lectures.  She is strong willed and respectable.  I have most certainly learned a thing or two from her class - rewarding indeed.

She really knows her stuff.  She might go a little fast at times, but will go around during class and tests to make sure you understand.  Teaching style is great and easy to understand, no confusion.  We need more instructors like her.

Very good class, excellent study framework.  The Syllabus is key. The class is an aggressive approach to to passing the exams and requires dedicated commitment to stick with the program.  Although I found it better than stretching the testing period over several months or longer. I passed 6 of the 7 tests in the 3 month time frame, well worth it.
Great and informative.  Very knowledgeable.  Although the class may be on a quick pace,  for the more detailed oriented, its a great help!Instructor is humbling :)

Dear Prof. Umber,   It was a pleasure meeting you. have a great combination of  qualities for teaching :The ability to transmit information in a straight forward way,  clear illustration on the computer  of the step by step process and enthusiasm for your subject.     Many Thanks!
great class.
Thanks so much!  You're class was great!  I enjoyed your teaching style and I look forward to having you as my ARE instructor. :) Tiffani Ward, NAVFAC SW
would definitely recommend.
Very inteligent. Covered a lot of ground and clearly knew her material.
Thanks so much!  You're class was great!  I enjoyed your teaching style and I look forward to having you as my ARE instructor. :)
Clear and succinct.
I WAS IMPRESSED ON DEPTH OF KNOWLEDGE AND EASY OF PRESENTATION.
Excellent class. Thank you!
Really mean
Excellent overview in preparation for the Mechanical/Electrical portion of the ARE.  This class, plus the recommended studying, should help anyone pass this monster of an exam.

Umber Kazmi is a truly gifted instructor for the architecture licensing exam.  She got me through this exam and I never could have done it without her help.  Her advice is invaluable and she really packs an enormous amount of information into a short amount of time.
Umber is good at what she does. thanks to her class i am excited to take all the exams seriously.
VERY GOOD
Class was great. The best part is the study methodolgy because it is a true guide to pass the ARE exam.

Very good at her subject, but students could use more time to explore materials w/ professor. Perhaps all three seminars need to be taken together to get a better understanding? (ME & CD w/PD, ARE 3.1) Adding question and answer session could be useful. Suggest taking PD last in examination order may help (if possible for non-transition candidate)
Very good class for getting the scope and basics of the ARE given the time alloted for the class.

Very professional. Clear concepts. Seminar time should extend somewhat more and divide the Predesign Seminar into the 5 categories. Enjoyed the Seminar, which allowed me to analize if additional studying was required or not.
You are the perfect instructor for post-college students - pace is quick but covers all topics.  Intellectual level at par with class levels.   Amazing!
Funkaar Studios has truly given me an upper hand in the preparation of the ARE 4.0.

Great Instructor. Speaks very fast because there's a lot of material to cover. She knows her stuff! I just wish she'd explain the info  briefly so we know what it's about because some students might not have the study materials on hand.
Very useful; A Brief introductino for the topics to be covered during each session and the context of eahc topic would be helpful before jumping in; Overall, worth the time an expense.
not enough material covered in depth
The class was 100% helpful. I have, and continue, to recommend the professor and Funkaar to all that will listen.
I highly recommend ... great starting point w/ lots of clarity ... worth the time to take the courses.

Great organization and structure.  Super good curse

Great Course!  Prof. Kazmi was very helpful in helping me prepare for the A.R.E. - I took Materials + Methods, Constr. Docs and Services, and Programming, Planning and Practice Prep Courses. I highly recommend these seminars - she hits it hard and fast and straight to the point!

Found the ARE prep course very helpful.  I took 8 of the tests at a rate of about 1 1/2 weeks apart and passed them all the first time around.  I don't think that would have been possible without her help.

THE FIRST 25 MINUTES OF THE CD LECTURE WAS USEFULL MATERIAL ON HOW TO APPROACH ANSWERING THE TEST.  ALTHOUGH A LOT OF RUSHED INFO FOR CD & PD, SHE IS KNOWLEDGABLE AND I'D TAKE HER EXPERIENCE OVER ANY OTHER LECTURE SERIES AS SHE HAS TAKEN AND PASSED ALL ARE EXAMS.

ARE exam prep course for Materials and Methods, Construstion Documents, MEP, and Building Technology.  Took course with high hopes, but ended up failing each exam.  Instructor had an attitude and spoke so quickly that it was very difficult to discern some of her vocabulary.
Prof. Kazmi, was extremely helpful in giving us the necessary tools in order to pass the ARE tests.  Loved the class, to the point, clear and intense.

Umber is a hardworking and disciplined person whom I admire very much. By the way, I don't know what the "class code is, so I wrote Hist 101.
very clear with alot of materal in a short period of time
Umber is the greatest. She lays out the material in a clear concise format that is easy to comprehend and remember. I passed all the exams first time thru
way to fast... way to much material to cover... better to take an online course from PREPARE (structures only) everything else has been useful from her.
Umber was very clear in all her explanations. She covers alot of material in a short amount of time. Doesn't waste any time and is very knowledgeable about the subject.

It's a lot of material in a short period of time.  Pre-prepare, pay attention, do your homework.  You'll pass.  Thank you Professor Kazmi.
The classes were phenominal in content and presentation. I've passed seven exams in eight weeks. Umber is the greatest.
Absolutely clear, fast, and helpful!  Thank yoU!

I went into the ARE class not knowing what to expect, and having almost no information on the process of taking the ARE's.  Umber was direct, informative, fast, and mostly clear on what to expect on the test.  I've been passing the exams using her techniques!
Classes have been very good and clear, as a preparation for the ARE.
I took the ARE Prep Courses she taught.  I found the courses extremely helpful. But buckle-up, she lectures fast because a ton of info needs to be covered.
very informative and helpful

Very textbook approach but the information is good.  Moves a bit fast....but the course was condensed so speed is important.  Can't represent that she has any particular enthusiasm for the material, but I would attend again because she gets the job done.  If I pass I will send her an e-mail and thanks to all the staff. Printed course material:good.
Good teacher.

Still haven't taken the test!

This was a fast-paced class and found the instructor's teaching method very useful.

very informative.

Very good class to take for the preperation of the ARE.  I took all nine courses, and felt all of them were a must.  Umber was very fast going through the content, and helpful when asked a question. LITTLE TOO FAST

she is ok

Umber is always professional and very knowledgeable- she sometimes doesnt have enough time in our sessions to go over everything. Great professor. Seems to anticipate all questions as she teaches. inspires confidence in the knowledge provided.
She is a plethera of information! She does goes through things quickly so you got to keep up and write fast.  Very informative.  I'm signing up for some tutoring with her!
very fast and to the point
Very helpful.  I like her methodology.

I thought the classes were very informative and would definitely recommend to others before taking the ARE.

I had her for GS and ME...great job.  She goes like lighting but is clear and focused. Study 4 hours a day...stick with it!

Umber, Is extremely helpful and covers a lot of material is a short time w/ clarity. I took her ARE classes for SP, BD and BT.
