no comments

Umber explains in very good detail... made General Structures and Lateral Forces much easier to understand with straight forward explanations and diagrams... have your brain prepare for a lot of information overload and learn to write short hand when taking notes... excellent handouts and study methodology given with class... thank you for teaching
Passed General Structures after failing twice prior.Still waiting to hear back on Technology......
Umber was great!  Fast, but the information seemed to stick!
ARE class - extremely helpful in material content and what is expected as well as relating different bodies of information.

I thought the class material provided was very helpfule. All the tips and tricks really helped me! The only thing that felt was lacking was time, we ran through it pretty quickly. Otherwise I am extremely satisfied with the prof.
very motivated and driven; style is very different from other classes in the university; much more art based than architecture; not the greatest listener and a little limiting, but very supportive.
Umber was extremely helpful and clear; I would recommend her to anyone.
Exposed me to a different side of architectural design that is not commonly taught at the university.  It was an extremely unique, creative and enjoyable course.

Extremely informative.  Might schedule the class with more breaks and more time to help absorb the material a little easier. Hard to listen take notes and absorb the information at the rate she is delivering the class.
Well organized and helpful. I would take a course from her again. Nice legs...
Great help with the somewhat quirky graphic exams-both Site Planning and Building Technology seminars for ARE were very helpful.
She's is the best instructor i've had for AIA seminars and even most of my college courses.  hope to learn more from her.

took her class for the CSE Oral Exam (for the CA. Architects License) and she did a good job covering the material.  very much to the point, there is not a moment wasted in the class.  the class was fast but well worth it.

She is very knowledgeable and to the point.  Wastes no time.  Material was presented clearly and comprehensively.  I recommend her highly for those who are studying for Lateral Forces.  The only thing I would suggest is to to slow down the speed a little bit so that everybody's up to speed with the teaching.

THE SEMINARS WERE QUITE GOOD.  UNFORTUNATELY THERE WAS ONE EXAM I DID NOT PASS (GENERAL STRUCTURES) BUT I DO KNOW THAT WAS MY OWN FAULT DUE TO NOT BEING ABLE TO KEEP UP WITH THE STRUCTURED STUDY GUIDE.  ALL OF THE INFORMATION GIVEN WAS WELL PRESENTED AND EFFIENCENT.  THANKS AGAIN FOR ALL OF YOUR HELP!
Professional, knowledgeable and clear.  All round an excellent teacher.
Very useful information.  I have not yet taken the exam.  I wish the class was not as short though.... because it feels as if we are rushing through the entire class....

It wasn't what I expected... some people wanted the subject matter slower... I wanted it faster.  I was bored, there was a lot of repetitive information and most of the subject matter were items that I had been using for years... but it not a reflection on the teacher.  She went over exactly what she was supposed... I just couldn't use much of it.

Professor was extremely detailed and effecient.  Our class section was changed over from a second teacher who felt he could not teach such a large class and it was apparent midway in the quarter he couldn't handle a small sized class as they fell from a week to two weeks behind our class. Prof.Umber was amazing and helpful-Hope she's back next qtr!
Decent teacher. Certianly knows her stuff, but goes through information very fast so make sure you pay attention.
Easy to understand and knows how to explain things well. Sometime she explains things well that we don't need to ask her the same questions again, which is good.
The instructor was knowledgable, but breezed through the material.
UMBER IS A VERY GOOD TEACHER.SHE IS AN INSPIRATION FOR ME TO GET MY LICENSE IN CALIFORNIA AFTER I READ HER RESUME AND ALL THE WORK SHE HAS PERFORMED.CONGRATULATIONS.
she is very easy to understand and she shows a lot  of interest in the subject.
Umber teaches in a manner that is very easy to understand. She works fast and makes the time go by quickly.  I understood everything she was layin' down

Umber is gifted on her feet.  She's thinks fast and expresses things clearly.  I passed all six exams I took after her class.
Very Knowledgable and Helpful in regard to understanding what is expected to pass the ARE. Highly recommended for anyone in the process of taking the exams!
Thanks for all the help
she was great! Nice clean lay-out of the program, even the structure class was more digestable after her course & I haven't taken a math class in about 12 yrs. Thanks

The classes are very useful in providing a schedule and outline for studying.  I have passed each exam to this point following the schedule (6 total.)  The classes gave me the motivation to finish my exams efficiently, much better than I was doing on my own.  Umber is very willing to assist you when things are unclear and provides a clear outline.

i took the M/E as well as 2 of the graphics classes.  i took the M/E exam shortly after.  i felt the class was beneficial to provide just another outlet for information and reference.  the graphics classes were also helpful but were slightly rushed and could stand to be longer.
The Building Technology seminar should be for 4-5 hours instead of the 3 hour seminar to cover the content in detail
Very helpful. Tough schedule to compete with at 1 test per week. But very helful especially for structures tests.
Very thorough. I definitely felt more confident and prepared for this exam (Site Planning) as compared to others. Thank you for all your help, and I will be signing up for future courses.
The information was very helpful
Umber’s superior understanding of the material helped me to learn with greater ease and enjoyment.
Very knowledgable in the subject and can teach it very well unlike most other teachers at NewSchool
Clear and in-depth presentation of materials, but goes a little quick at times.
She is simply one of the best.
A very clear teaching style.  Her Study sessions were better then the class.
Umber has a very concise method, a genuine passion for teaching and is truly interested in the development of her students.
Umber is one of the best professors at the NSAD.  She explains the subject until everybody in class understands.  She is very helpful outside of class too. Thank you!
A well respected professor all around.  Encourages students to go forth with what she teaches.
On top of her game.  She is not only smart but a very good teacher as well.
Not only is she hot, but she's extremely smart, patient, and great at communicating.
very communicative

Ubmer was so helpful, I did not really understand a thing at the beginning but the handouts and the extra help outside of class was amazing.  she had a busy schedule and still found time to see us if we need her which all of us did. She would be wonderful addition to any school.
very well organized and professional, provided us with the tools needed to comprehend a difficult subject mater

unfair on grading, testing, and unwillingness to answer questions
The homework load is extremely heavy and not practical or useful. And he communicates poorly with students . DO NOT taking this class(but no choice as an MSBA student)
Midterm and finals are too difficult
Horrible in communication and graded unfairly
Horrible professor
Tough class overall. His reading lecture slides and hand gestures weren't helpful.
Tough class, with no PPT, no Word, only hard-to-decipher handwriting. Only teach hard proof of statistical inference. Unfriendly grading, average test score below 60.
worst professor in Columbia university. No cheat sheet on exams. Given only 10% A.
Taking his class is a waste of time. If you don't trust me, go take it.
Rude and impolite

Took Probability with him. If this is your first time, don't take the course, run away. If you want a light refresher, fair exams and a professor that will provide good insights behind the intuition of the theorems, this is your guy.
The worst professor I have seen in Columbia University. Never prepared the lectures. Very confused. But, he does not assign homeworks (If you think that is a good news)
Very confusing, unclear. The top three words used in class is 'ok' 'alright' 'so'. Maybe a good scholar, but not a good teacher. Don't take this class.

Lectures rooted in personal opinions rather than historical or current fact/provable truth. Doesn't tolerate people questioning his viewpoints, creating a hostile classroom. Has a clear personal/political agenda and shows disdain for disagreers. Preaches socialism while seemingly fundamentally misunderstanding it (like father like son)
The ego is simply outrageous, but still not as bad as his grading.

Major Debates featured a non-debatable topic presented by a socialist who is truly a capitalist feeding on the naivetés of college students. Prof. Mamdani practiced selective capitalism at its finest   making students buy a useless book Define and Rule that he never referenced throughout the entire course! I would not attend his courses again!

Prof. Azizi Othman's Computer Science class at Columbia University was incredibly supportive. As a handicapped student, I felt included and accommodated throughout the course. The lectures were clear, and the professor was always approachable. Highly recommend

While Mr. Othman's course is highly praised, I personally struggled to fully enjoy it due to my ADHD. The lectures were rich with content, but the fast-paced discussions and extensive readings were challenging for me to keep up with. While the course wasn't an easy experience for me, I appreciated his effort to ensure every student could succeed

Mr Othman is a gem! His passion for Computer Science is contagious, and he makes complex topics feel approachable. Always available to help, he inspires us to think critically and innovate. Truly a mentor!

reviews.comment: I think he is nice, disagree with the previous comments.

WORST professor ever. Had no passion within his teachings, used weird analogies to describe everything, seemed to not care at all about his students or their success and wanted to purposefully make everything more difficult for them. Teaching methods got progressively worse over the weeks until it was too late.
Avoid this class at all cost. Take Musatti or Gulati

Professor Ortega is absolutely incredible! They focus on trauma informed care which is right up my ally and we learned SO MUCH in our 14 weeks of class. You SHOULD NOT get your MSW from Columbia without taking a class with Professor Ortega, for REAL!

Prof Ortega is amazing. She is well-informed, well-intentioned and instructs with care and positive intentions. I think she is a rockstar and should teach HBSE-I forever. I walked away from the course with knowledge that will guide me in life and in my career.

Wonderful professor. Genuinely cares about understanding human behavior in varied contexts. LOVE her teaching style, creative, genuine caring. Diverse and relevant topics. Fully engaged. NEVER BORED. As a non-traditional student in my 50's, I am impressed with her vast knowledge. Best professor. Don't leave Columbia and not experience her class.

Professor Ortega is hands-down one of the best professors I have ever had in my life. She is knowledgable, caring, and helpful. She treats you like an adult and expects you to know your material and reading. She challenges your thinking. I absolutely recommend taking a class with her if you can. You will not regret it.
SHE IS AMAZING. YOU WILL LEARN AND TAKE AWAY SO MUCH FROM THIS CLASS. ASSIGNMENTS ARE ENLIGHTENING AND EASY TO FINISH.
Pedagogic style all over the place
Mere ideological preaching but no material practise of her politics
Pedagogy needs to be refined in tandem with oratorship
The reading list is lengthy, syllabus is changed frequently, which is expensive in terms of procuring texts
Get ready for emails that are never replied to

Its funny the way aesthetics play a part in life; Paige West loves to play the theatrical role of a professor who is floundering yet sharp, hip yet dying for people to acknowledge her “niceness,” projecting intellectual rigour while her pedagogy is rife with laziness.
Kind of lukewarm intellectually

West is a classic colonial-minded professor who performs theory with students in her inner circle who look like her. She claims to center marginalized voices, yet rewards privileged “bleeding-heart” students who theorize the oppressed while dismissing marginalized students speaking about their own lives.

She really cares about her students and is understanding, which was so valuable considering the pandemic and online classes. She is also really passionate about anthropology and knows what shes talking about.

She makes everyone feel seen and genuinely cares about your learning. She's an inspiring human being and someone I look up to. Very approachable, welcoming, understanding, and communicative.

Priya is one of the most caring professors I've encountered at CSSW. She can come off as a bit eccentric in the beginning but I'm grateful to have stuck it out with her. She has a unique approach to teaching and she taught the class so much. Some of the assignments weren't that productive in my opinion, but other than that I loved her class.

Professor Seshan is a very caring, passionate woman with a plethora of experience. She uses intriguing case studies from her life for class discussions. Must do weekly readings; they're interesting, though. Assignment prompts were a bit convoluted & most students needed to use office hours in order to grasp them. Very tough, but did learn a lot.
Very approachable and manageable class! Great lecturer always a pleasure to go to his lectures. Do homework & pay attention in lecture and you will be fine!
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: All around a great lecturer. He does plenty of cool tricks and offers a lot of extra credit opportunities. However, tests are tricky and typically not curved.
The labs are really cool plus he does magic tricks!
Took several classes with him during my time at Columbia. Loved his lectures.

This was one of the most boring and unengaging professors I have ever taken. I didn't know that film of the 1990s to present could be so incredibly painful. He didn't appreciate feedback that didn't match his own view and dismissed it if you don't "get it". I'm a film major and what he was seeing was so far out there it didn't make sense.

The most amazing professor I've ever had, honestly. He's so well spoken and he can answer any question you have about film history or really anything it seems. The films he picks are amazing, take his class!!!!

Prof. Pena is a living encyclopedia of film history. His choice of films is very unique and aims for broaden the horizon of the students. Short paper for each film per week. He will provide very productive comments and feedback.

This is a really tough class.  He is notoriously tough grader but what's worse is amount of writing.  A paper due every week, plus a midterm and final 10+ pages each.  Major workload, but really, it's worth it.  He is a feather in the columbia film cap and his film knowledge is second to none. If you come through CU, u have to take him.

Pena is a difficult guy to pin down - he has an encyclopaedic knowledge of anything film, from anywhere in the world.  But I don't think he's very good interacting one-on-one, and he can sometimes be very bad about responding to emails.  But overall, he will challenge you, expand your horizons and give you a good class experience.

Pena was the worst professor I've ever had, which is saying a lot.  On the rare occasions that he'd allow students to talk, he was condescending and dismissive, refusing to hear any viewpoint but his own.  His lectures were tedious, his assignments unnecessarily copious, and his comments lazy and uninterested.  D-

Knows cinema and the business inside and out - makes every movie interesting through lecture and discussion and readings, invites discussion and responds thoughtfully, creates challenging and interesting assignments, reads all the papers you turn in and makes lots of comments, funny, really nice guy

reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Amazing lectures

Professor Sen is extremely knowledgable in her field, as well as a passionate educator. She is very flexible, willing to talk with students and change her class/teaching style accordingly, as well as provide individual feedback. Professor Sen is a great lecturer, and provides context for her arguments that makes them easy to follow.

Favorite Professor I've had thus far as a graduate student here at Columbia. Passionate about the subject, highly knowledgeable, and invested in her students' success, she stands apart from other professors.

tough grader, don't know how to make students understand, but only showing his academic ability

Regret taking this guy's course so much even though I survived. He goes through things so fast that you wonder whether he actually wants you to understand them or not; and you can hear half of the class sighing most of the time due to inability to follow. Lots of homework, tough problems, not learning much. YOU ARE WARNED: Avoid him at all costs.

Really nice guy, I think almost to a fault. Explains concepts clearly and answers literally every single question in class. Sometimes he moves too quickly and sometimes too slowly, but I found the homework for this class to be really useful and was good practice.

Has no idea how to ask questions. His HW's are long and you learn absolutely nothing. Worst professor I've ever had in my life, would give him a negative rating if I could, I want to apologize to the rest of my teachers I've had in my life, because compared to this guy they seem like amazing teachers.

Stuart Ward is a nice faculty. His course covers major knowledge points on statistics, using concrete examples to illustrate formulas. Stuart has patience to answer students' questions from different background

A wonderful professor who genuinely cares for his students. He is knowledgeable, intelligent, and makes himself available anytime, and anywhere (dedicated to our success both within the classroom and in our professional lives). I highly recommend - a lifetime access to an incredible human being with incredible expertise in statistics.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: One of the best professors I've ever had. Highly knowledgable on the material, makes himself available outside of class, and is extremely approachable.
Great professor
labs and lectures are long. monotonous. expect  a lot of homework and labs. practice practice practice for the exam. unclear lectures  of a very tough subject.
Amazing teacher with extremely helpful lectures.
I walked into the class terrified and left ready to take another class with Stu! Absolutely recommend his Statistics course.

Best Quantitative Analysis professor I ever had. Before attending Columbia I took Quant at NYU. Profeesor Ward taught me more in two weeks than I learned in an entire semester at NYU. He is not the run of the mill conventional professor. But his methods are effective and my learning has been sustained for 9 years.

EMPA U6311.001 QUANTITATIVE TECHNIQUES I & II    Very smart professor, very encouraging, very personable/approachable, but the [required] courses were a waste of time for anyone with any Stats experience.  Passing exams requires wrote memorization and very little comprehension.  Try to test out.
Not focused

Horrible lecturer. His lectures were like him rambling and he is super unhelpful to help prepare for the midterms. He only has one practice midterm and when asked for what asked we could do, he said the midterm was going to be just like the practice. It was nothing like the practice and he doesn't offer drops/extra credit.
Calculus III is a tricky subject. Professor Wang is a really nice guy who is willing to help though!
He reads off of slides of the textbook. Useless lecture. Extremely difficult exams.
Professor Sala acts more like an entertainer than a teacher. Engaging lectures but little learning. He also quite political and promotes a pretty extreme view on neoliberalism.
good, funny professor
Incredibly interesting AND entertaining. Most useful material I've learned in school. Midterm comically easy, final difficult but with heavy curve. Absolute must take!
Yale's great loss to Columbia. The best professor I had ever had at Yale.

The best lecturer I've ever had, and he has fantastic TAs that are extremely helpful. He is interesting, informative, and his tests are right out of his homework problems. Bless this man. And I agree with the previous post, take any class you can with him!
Best Professor I've ever had. He's smart, funny, cute (VERY CUTE!) and lovely (VERY LOVELY). I love this man!!!
Take any class from this man if you have the chance.
fantastic teacher, brilliant man
AWESOME PROFESSOR
No Comments
brilliant guy who can actually teach
No Comments
Entertaining, but he doesn't really teach you anything.
Best Teacher I've ever had.
Professor or comedian???
No Comments
David Yerkes is the best thing about Columbia, no question.

Dr. Yerkes is not everyone's cup o' tea... but he s got a keen intellect and his main purpose is to make you THINK!  If you think you can drift, lazily, through his class you would be wrong.  The man has been doing this for almost 40 years and knows his stuff!  Is he a hard grader?  Not particularly, but this is not an "easy A".  I enjoy him, YMMV!
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Get ready to read--Hilarious

reviews.comment: Quit caring quite a while ago, he's got the tenure & has checked out. I had him for Hist. of American Lang. which was an awful course.  He basically adopts Pinker's ideas as his own & ridicules the "lesser" Bryson. It would be one thing if Yerkes devoted himself to research (he hasn't--he's been harping on the OED for the last 10 yrs--no book yet).
AVOID AT ALL COSTS. History of English Language is a complete joke.  He didn't even have a syllabus. Nasty & grades erratically.
worst teacher EVER
No Comments

His class is well and humorously taught. Yerkes has little time for students without interest looking for a gut course, but his knowledge pays off if you pay attention.  His Hist. of the Eng. Lang class also made passing the Univ. of Oxford M.Phil qualifying exam in Philology a breeze, by the way.
No Comments
Great prof. Quirky as heck, but a keen intellectual who teaches through ancedotes. Stay focused, relax, and you'll learn a lot.

I took her for SOCW T713A (Proposal Development) and she made the subject easy to understand. She is a excellent professor. She was always open to questions and very flexible. There is A LOT of mandatory group work and was a tough grader, but always constructive and fair. I would definitely take again!

Professor Marti is one of the best professors I've ever had. She respects her students, provides clear direction and expectations for assignments, and is incredibly approachable. Professor Marti is energetic and passionate. She is always open to answering any and all questions students may have, both about her class and any other topic. Wonderful!

Prof. Marti is excellent! She works hard to present the material as clear as possible and at the same time engaging. She is there if you need help, and assistance but expects you to pull your own weight. She's one of those professors that demands quality work and in the end your thankful for it. I wish I could take another class of hers!!!
wouldn't recommend but knowledgeable about mathematics

Really friendly guy, always excited to dig into real-world examples for topics that are honestly otherwise boring. Zhang somehow made calc I something that I looked forward to attending.

Mostly lecture-style, but willing to stop for questions.

All of the textbook sections for each lecture are on the website, so you can catch up when you're sick.
I took Calculus I in his first time teaching. A lot of motivation and connections to medicine, economics, physics, and all kinds of other real life problems!
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: I've taken a few classes with Pat and they're really what you make of them. She gives the framework for you to take off with the material and produce work or you can also just sit back and blow it off, but you'll be bored beyond belief. She gives great lesson ideas but beware of any class with trips or too much media-hella disorganizedincohesive.

Zumhagen, like most TC faculty, play the charade of the academic but fail to conduct classes that are any more challenging than what you would find at your typical community college. Her lame air of theatricality would only convince the most naive of students.
The BEST. Amazingly fair, interesting, gives great assignments, cares about teaching, models wonderful teaching strategies in the classroom. A real star. Recommend highly.
Brilliant fun interesting. The best teacher I have had at Columbia by far.
Pat Zumhagen is a fabulous professor.  She's a gifted and creative teacher, always clear and very knowledgable and supportive.  I highly recommend her courses.
An absolutely amazing lady.  There's not much else that can be said.  I'm sure she'll be fabulous at Columbia.
Aaron's class was awesome, but kept me busier than a moth in a mitten!
I didn't expect to like this class as much as I did. It's like they say, "a mule can be tame at one end and wild at the other."

Taking his class in American Culture and the Corporate novel. Creative and dynamic professor who will consistently blow your mind with his supplemental readings and connections to the novel at hand. He really knows how to lead a discussion that pulls all the students in. Its like the greatest book club ever. Im auditing so I cant speak to grades
It's a hard class if you're not really a writer but you'll learn that even a bull without horns can still do some solid pushing.

Low grader: I got a 79.45 course average and he gave me a C not a B. However, he is the smartest professor I know when it comes to business and computer science. I recommend him even though he grades on the low side.

Good professor. Knows his stuff. Very knowledgeable. Exams are difficult. Gives lots of homework but not enough time during class to go over it. Takes attendance and marks you absent if 15 minutes late.

Great professor. He teaches the material clearly. He knows his stuff very well. Makes you work hard for your grade. If you have an 89 average he will give you a B, not an A. If you miss a class you really miss a lot. Can't slack off in his class.
Excellent professor, knows his stuff, gives hard tests
I had two classes with Professor Ahmar, and she continued to help me with reading feedback beyond the classes. I recommend her class.

I did not expect to realistically improve that much in such a short time.  If you follow all assignments, and pay attention in class, you will get more than one level of improvement in a very short time.  I recommend this class to anyone serious about their education.
If you are serious about the subject, this professor is your best bet. She is an expert and will do her best for you to get the material.

I took this research class almost 5 years ago. The class changed my life after leaving the university. I used everything that was taught in this class to submit and win a pretty large research grant. Now I am in a PhD and everything that was covered in SW research is extremely helpful to me even at the PhD level. Hat's off to Dr. Ada!
You won't regret!
The most amazing Professor!

Professor Mui is very knowledgeable and has a very interesting history. Videos in class were interesting as well. No exams, just a lit review, then part A of your proposal, then part b, followed by the group presentations at the end of class.

Ada is a great professor with a deep background in research. She refers to her own research in aging a lot so if you want to work with the aging population that's a bonus. Very understanding if you need an extension for a good reason. SO FUNNY. So far an easy grader but the TA has done most of the grading. Would take another class with her!

Professor Mui is a warm and funny woman. I took her class at night and she always made sure the class was lively. All the work is group work and as along as you pick good group members, you will be sure to get a good grade. Professor Mui uses her research and past student work as examples to display what she wants so that you are never confused.

Taking SW Research with Professor Ada was great! You choose a group to work with for the duration of the semester and work on a research paper. She helps you throughout the term and calls these research papers "growing babies". As long as you get along with your group members and do equal work, you should get a good grade. Prof. Ada is a huge help!

Great instructor with a lot of work and life experience to share. She is very passionate about what she does and uses humor to solidify her teaching points. She is a breath of fresh air and a delight to have as an instructor.

She is a really sweet, and funny professor who is genuinely interested in getting her students inspired about Research. I had little interest and still do about the topic but overall it was an easy class. The first day of class we were split into groups to do our big research proposal which she refers to as our "babies" haha.

Professor Blazej is the best ! He goes out of his way to help you. Going into this class I thought it was going to be really hard but I was wrong. The class was awesome!!! The material was fun to learn and Professor Blazej helped me out whenever I needed him. He was easy to get a hold of and he responded to all of my e-mails asap.

Prof Blazej was easily one of the best instructors I had at Columbia. The class that I took with him was essentially just an intro to philosophy course, which he made quite interesting and enjoyable. He was able to condense a huge amount of topics and reading into a manageable and engaging course, making fair accommodations for his students.

Prof Blazej was an absolutely amazing professor, especially as an into to the concepts and thinkers of philosophy. His dedication and care for each student is evident and he takes the time to talk with you about any questions/progress. I appreciated his unique and collaborative assignments. He adjusted to and took advantage of the online format.

Adam Blazej is a phenomenal professor. If you have the opportunity to take a class from him, I highly recommend doing so. Professor Blazej is great at facilitating conversation, makes lessons interesting, and is always happy to answer questions or further explain an idea. He clearly cares a great deal about the subject matter and his students.

This is the second course I have taken with Professor Blazej and he is fantastic! I would take every class with him if I could. His teaching style and etiquette draws you into every lecture and makes you want to read and learn the material. He ensures the class is engaged with all aspects of the lecture and loves to converse with his students.

Professor Blazej is a fantastic teacher! He is extremely personable and easy to talk to. He makes every lecture pleasant and engaging. He is able to translate the material in a way that is understandable and relatable. This was the first Lit Hum course and I had ever taken and Professor Blazej made it so enjoyable I took another one of his courses.

Adam Blazej is without a doubt one of my favorite professors that I've been fortunate enough to work with during my time at Columbia University. His class was one that I always looked forward to each week last spring. Adam is a truly inspirational person and deeply cares about his students. I only wish I had the opportunity to take more from him!!!

I took Professor Blazej for two semesters at Columbia university, and would take his class again if I could. As a science major, I didnt have a background in Philosophy going in, but left feeling confident having a conversation about the topic with anyone. He respects students and made class feel like a group of friends getting together.

Prof Blazej is one of my all-time favorite teachers. He navigated heated discussions and always guided us when needed. I deeply appreciate his teaching style: he does not tell us the answers but makes us work for them. He says things like "I don't understand this, maybe you can help me get clear on xyz..." He is brilliant, humble and very caring.
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Get ready to read--Caring

reviews.comment: Prof. Blazej is an exceptional educator and has my utmost respect. His classes are truly enjoyable and thought-provoking. Additionally, he communicates his expectations clearly and grades assignments very reasonably. Lastly, he has very gracious extra credit and deadline redemption policies. Take one of his classes, I guarantee you won't regret it!

Adam Blazej was easily amongst my favorite professors at Columbia University; he is approachable, non-pretentious, and fun. He understands how to introduce beginners to Philosophy while remaining intellectually engaging with the more seasoned students. You'll learn a ton in class while also gaining outside support. Workload/grading fair and unique.

She is a wealth of knowledge! She knows her stuff and attendance to in class is not mandatory which is awesome if you already know how to manage your time. she gives ungraded quizzes that test material similar to the exam. Do the work and you will easily get an A!
I would take any class that Dr. Aboelela teaches. She is an amazing, extraordinary professor. It is an honor and privilege to be able to learn under her.
she is cool

I LOVE Sally's class. She's one of the most intelligent and caring prof in CUSON. Yes it is content heavy, yes lecture is long, and yes it is test heavy wky quiz and 3 exams. But the classes she's teaching are supposed to lay foundation for nursing and is hard by its nature. She is really good at explaining things and her test questions are fair.

Prof is 'phoning it in'; does the bare min..  Teaches to Study Guides which don't reflect the exam or quiz content.  Uses old videos, filled with background noise.  Impossible to get a hold of, and her exam format is cruelly 'tricky', esp. for a pre-req course. Woefully out of touch. TA also poor; googling answers; was expecting more from Columbia

Sally is very caring, and super intelligent. She's great at explaining complex topics from the ground up. Pharm & microbio are always going to be dry & heavy because it's just so much information. Patho is where she really shines. Her lectures are long, her content is heavy, her classes are difficult, but I found it fairly easy to get an A or A-
Sally is one of the best professors I had at Columbia University; highly recommend her courses!

Feelings aside, this is the worse class I have ever taken! Prof. A should NOT be teaching Pharmacology since she isn't a pharmacist to begin with. Cramming is her outdated teaching style. 

Weekly quizzes, 3 exams, 3-4 hrs long pre-recorded lectures, then 3 hrs of live online class, PLUS 2+ hours of office hrs because the material wasn't finished.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Dr. A is one of the most intelligent, informative, & kind professors I’ve encountered at CU. I strongly disagree w/ the reviews below. As graduate nursing student I have had Dr. A for both N7001&2 & have found her to be extremely flexible, organized, & concise. She provides thorough knowledge of topics while still keeping the workload manageable

Surprised to see the negative reviews. She is one of the most intelligent individuals I have ever met. Her lectures are extremely thorough and she does a great job explaining topics from the ground up. Material can be heavy at times but she makes it tolerable. Exams are extremely fair and she provides study guides. Wonderful learning experience.

One of the worst professors I have ever had. The lectures are difficult to follow and the exams are impossible to study for. She speaks in circles and the tangents make the substance of the lectures mean almost nothing. I was forced to take classes with her for three semesters and I wouldn't wish it on my worst enemy.

Dr. A is neither clear nor concise. Her class is four hours long and she is not able to speak clearly about the subject matter. Her thought process is circumstantial on a good day, and her exams are impossible to study for because she is not clear wile she is teaching the material. I stopped attending the lectures and googled her study questions.

Dr. A is a lovely person, but speaks in mind bending riddles and manages to complicate things that I entered her class understanding. She talks for about 45 minutes about something that could easily be summarized in two to three sentences. She really understands and knows her stuff, but my learning style and her teaching style do not mix.

Disclaimer: non-nursing student so I cannot speak for her graduate nursing courses. For A&P, Dr. Aboelela taught us well. She's overall a very kindhearted person and very understanding. She always helped clarify any concepts we had trouble with and made complex material in physiology actually digestible.

Nice person, horrible professor. Expects us to watch 3+ hours a week of outside lecture videos and to attend 2-3 hour long synchronous classes where she talks about material that is not covered in the lecture videos. Her exams are illegible and she often goes over time or adds in abundant last minute material to study. Unorganized and annoying.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Graded by few things--Test heavy

reviews.comment: This is not the right professor to be teaching any type of patho physiology or pharmacology. Too monotone and extremely vague with her style of questioning / expectations of students. If you have this professor for either course, be prepared to learn on your own. She doesnt even respond to emails (I guess shes too good to help her students).
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Such a disappointing professor. Seems confused by her own material. Her powerpoints are not organized coherently and riddled with errors. her lectures are tedious and slow.

Nice lady. Fun-ish personality. Voice is very slow and can be monotone, doesn't help when learning patho or pharm. In general her lectures are completely disorganized and she focuses a lot on the neuro aspect of EVERYTHING. Tests vaguely reflect what she teaches in class. Nice person for sure, not a good teacher at all.

Just a fantastic lecturer and so, so knowledgeable. She has a uniquely calming voice and her enthusiasm for the material is contagious. I listen to her lectures at 1.5 or 1.75x when I playback on ECHO to study, but in class her slow speech is very helpful when learning new topics. Cannot recommend her enough.

Sally makes normally complicated subjects easy to follow! Her lecture style is like none other because she lectures straight from memory (with visual aids) rather than by reading of slides (we've all had those professors before). She goes through each topic slowly and makes everything so clear. Everyone who had Sally before loves her.

Overall, the class material is difficult. However, she is amazing. She is really engaging and really really helpful. I feel blessed to have her teaching a difficult topic. She really tries to break it down into the most simplistic way possible. For reference, I've had her for pharm and patho 1 & 2.
A memorably bad teacher.
Why is this person in the classroom?
No Comments
No Comments

They say that gaining proficiency in a language requires training four skills: listening, speaking, and (to also have literacy) reading and writing. If you take a class with Aili, you'll see at least eight!

Aili Flint is an excellent professor.  Any person is a privilege to meet Aili and interact with her.  She is a highly skilled, knowledgeable, and dedicated teacher.  The course is difficult, but well worth the effort.  Aili is also personally enthusiastic about music, art and mythology with was enriching to my Columbia experience.
No Comments

She is totally committed to her subject (Finnish language and literature) and to her students. She goes to superhuman lengths to be helpful to her students. Aili is an outstanding, highly effective teacher and a wonderful human being.
The best Professor I've had, regardless of subject!  Professor Blaer taught Physics that even a layman can understand.  Very rare teacher in today's world.
Alan is a legent and by far the best teacher I ever had. I took 2 courses in Electromagnetism with him in 1982-83 and I still remember and use his lecture....
The best the school has to offer.
THE BEST I CLASS I EVER TOOK

Professor Blaer is one of the best teachers I have ever had.  His lectures are incredibly clear and he is tremendously helpful both as a lecturer and at his office hours.  I have no hesitation in giving him my highest recommendation.  Columbia is lucky to have him, he is a great teacher who really cares about students.
2 words: THE BEST!!! Not only is he an excellent teacher, he's SUUUUUUUUPPPPPEEEEEEEERRRRRRRRRR nice, and he looks so adorable..u just wanna pinch his cheeks!
Take a class with him and I promise you won't regret it.
One of the warmest, cleverest, clearest, and most helpful people anybody is likely to meet.
ABSOLUTELY THE BEST!!!!!!!
He is the man.

High level of innovation came from her pedagogy from many of her students. The students who are weak and came with an attitude of entitlement had difficulty with the unique challenges professor chun instilled.
Alice Chun has a proven track record of stealing her student's ideas and trying to sell them or pass them off as her own Stay away!
Very unclear person. She does not explain what she wants from you, and expects a lot of work, giving minimum explanations.

Alice largely rests her pedagogy on her ideas of 'material'. Unlike other  "materially thoughtful" profs such as Karla Rothstein, Alice's ideas of materiality offer little more than a insight on emotional consequences. if you like warm+fuzzy, take Alice, if you want intellectual insight, look elsewhere

I wanted to like Professor Albert. But the guy unfortunately has no respect for others. He frequently interrupts students and auditors of the class who are often just his age and stature. He loves to hear his own voice. Grades were completely arbitrary and attending lectures/doing very few readings does not help. Most of the class is about physics.

Despite other reviews, he's not arrogant. He's opinionated, but if you don't take it personally you'll discover that he's teaching you *how* to think, not *what* to think. While he tends to talk over people, just be patient. He truly cares about helping you understand the content. I learned more from him than I ever have from any other professor.
interesting

Direction of Time is one of the two really memorable courses I took at Columbia.  Course contents aren't easy, but he really cares about teaching and is very down to earth.  He makes difficult concepts easy to understand and does not put up a front by trying on false modesty- he speaks his mind.
Awful.  Arrogant and completely out of touch with students.

Terrible, terrible, terrible, smug, little self-important "man" wholly incapable of either writing or uttering one coherent sentence.  The man just babbles one disconnected thought after another.  He earned his degree in physics but does nothing in that field, hence why he turned to philosophy and is in the phil dept. Boring and an uncaring weirdo.

I have no idea why Columbia doesn't fire him.  He has a reputation for being totally disconcern for teaching, which he is genuinely horrible at doing.  He clearly doesn't care about his students.  Just a terrible "professor."  And his books he himself wrote are equally horribly poor in quality.  I wish I knew about him before taking the course.

The man simply cannot teach to save his life.  For the Direction of Time course, he used his own text, a horribly unreadable text.  He is also arrogant and not at all available.  No email replies or real feedback.  He's not a bad guy but has no business teaching--or writing.

This guy is awesome. He makes the material lively and down to earth, and responds really helpfully to student questions. Among the best few teachers I have had. His presentation of the topic is definitely one-sided, but that doesn't have to be a problem - go read a (different) book and try the counterarguments on him, if you want to learn the max.
do the reading and you will enjoy this kickass prof!
This was a philosophy of physics (quantum) that I dropped...subject material is fascinating, but his book is awful, and his lectures just reiterate the book.
Pretty middle of the road, all around.  His lectures conformed a bit too closely to the text.

What you receive depends on how open you are. Excellent arguments which can be followed even if you don't agree with him. He forced many, at the threshold of growing out of their cocoon, to transform themselves, and that's what matters in some courses more than the contents. He handled this SUPERBLY.
Crucial part of my growing up!
He is the worst professor I ever had in my college. He also taught at IIT Bombay where the undergrads disliked him with passion.
one of the shining lights of the philosophy department. sometimes it's a challenge to follow his very high-level arguments, but it's very worth the effort.

Hassan was an excellent Professor. He was ideal for this class, as he was able to tutor me not just on a theoretical level, but also using his expertise as a Career Mastermind. I highly recommend that Hassan teach other classes. We need Professors that truly care, and are not apathetic (I have found that some are). Thank you Hassan!!

Brilliant Professor! One of the last pieces of advice (of many) he gave me before graduating was that your greatest asset is your current job, as you will be using it as a bridge job for the next job and it will help market you (along with references you align); protect it, leverage it, maximize it! He rocks! He should teach more classes!!!!!!!!

Of all the professors I have taken at Columbia, he is the most fresh and innovative. What he says MAKES SENSE, and gives analogies that are really invaluable If I wouldn't have taken this class, I would have been lost, as my career track isn't that straightforward and he made it clear for me. He prepares you for the "future of meaningful work".

A must take professor at the Sch of Professional Studies. He teaches you about personal branding and positioning in your career, as well as playing well in the sandbox. That your current job is your biggest asset as it makes you more marketable and serves as a bridge job. Loved the course and him. They are lucky to have him!

He should be teaching leadership, one of my favorite professors of all time! Take his class! He motivated me to not second guess myself and add true meaning to my career/life vision. He shared a framework on how to align my purpose and I have never gotten that kind of career coaching before. No surprise he has so many thank you cards in his office!

Prof Sagiv is definitely my favorite math professor of all time, and I miss attending his lectures.  He explained all concepts very thoroughly. For me the deal breaker was the Proctorio system of test taking which malfunctioned during both exams and consumed valuable test time.  I now attend a different school that doesn't use Proctorio!

ngl I self-studied everything by reading the textbook. The quality of the recording was horrible, could barely see anything on the board. Weekly hw takes around 10 to 15 hours for me to finish but it's only 20% of the total grade, midterm is 30% and final is 50%. So if you're not good at taking exams (like me) then your hard work is worth nothing.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Avg final grade is 79.1, which is a B. In our major, you need an avg of B to graduate. He thinks students should learn "at least half" on their own (said this himself). Maybe that's just his excuse for the poor lecture quality? And teaching urself is hard, when 3 textbooks are used mixed up. Got an A-, but still super confused about most topics.

WORST EVER. Filled with weird notations and useless talks, his 1hr lecture isn't even close to most of the 15-min YouTube videos, which go over the exact same topics and points. Without YouTube, many would die halfway thru the semester. U are expected to teach most of the content to yourself through internet. Simply, a useless professor.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read

reviews.comment: Prof. Hielscher has moved to NYU, and I took a class with him there. He is an exceptional professor and teacher. His years of teaching experience show in his positive interactions with students. I highly recommend his courses!
Everything you ever wanted to know about biophotonics. Includes current research topics. Great class with hands-on programming labs.
Excellent introductory biomedical imaging course. Youll learn all the basics and so much more. Highly recommended!
Best class on numerical methods in biophotonics. You'll learn all the basic and advanced stuff needed.
You'll learn a lot.
Excellent introduction to medical imaging. Highly recommended! Prof knows his stuff.
It is somewhat hard - but you will learn a lot. Very worthwhile course.
Great course - great teacher

Professor Claybaugh has left Columbia, and joined the Englisg Dept. at Harvard- Columbia's loss is definitely Harvard's gain! This woman is brilliant, and manages to convey her brilliance in a stimulating, yet non overbearing way. Her knowledge of her subject, and enthusiasm for it, are tremendous, as is her availability and responsiveness. A+++!
Fabulous!
Brilliant, but demands the very best work. Impossible to slack off.
What an incredibly boring woman.  She is so proud of her intelligence it's impossible to think of anything but her own superciliousness.
No Comments
Prof. Claybaugh is one of the most intelligent teachers I've encountered and continues to express some of the most unique/intriguing thoughts concerning the material.
No Comments
No Comments

God was he awful! The most pompous, self-absorbed professor at P&S. He has a gift for taking straightfowrd material and making it complicated....and his attitude toward female students is just plain creepy.
Dr. April is actually a pretty cool professor. As a first year medical student I had heard horror stories but found that most of them were exaggerated. Overall I would enjoy having him again.

OMG. This guy is still teaching?! I took his class back in 1990-1 and hated it. The man was generally disliked by P&S students back then. He was consistently hitting on the more atractive women in the class. Very ego driven. A legend in his own mind.

Dr. April certainly thinks he is one of the best teachers at the medical school, but he is actually one of the worst.  His approach to teaching is best described as Machievellian.  Whatever you do, don't go to his office hours.
He repeatedly says to come to him for extra help, but when you do, he is completely unhelful.  Not someone I'd want to be trapped in an elevator with.
As first year professors go, he is pretty clear, his ego is n't too bad, and he usually avoids esoteric diversions.  He's not going to be your best bud, but so what.
truly horrendous.  it has been more than three years, and i'm still recovering.
arrogant, not very helpful.
favorite first year class

Dr. April is actually a really cool guy.  He may come off as a little gruff, but he's really not.  He enjoys teaching anatomy and tries to make the course interesting and clinically relevant.  People who have trouble with anatomy like to take their frustration out by bashing him.
THE WORST TEACHER IN THE WORLD.  AND MEAN-SPIRITED GUY TOO.  BUT HIS COURSE IS REQUIRED.

Professor April is an exceedingly insecure, vulgar individual who consistently treats students in an inappropriate manner.  He sometimes fabricates anecdotes during his lectures, which also contain highly inappropriate and bigotted jokes.  Anatomy is fascinating, in spite of his teaching.
Very professional and knowledgeable
I liked him.
Ari is a great professor. His style is different than many at the J-School. He's very helpful if you go to him.
No Comments

Horrible. His lectures are full of just anecdotes and poorly made slides. The entire class is graded based on two papers only with very poorly written instructions. Avoid his classes if at all possible.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: He was amazing in the classroom, very dynamic and engaging. Once we moved online (due to covid), a little less theatrical, and our class was not as engaged. Fairly light class, two short papers. I enjoyed the course overall and would recommend if you are looking for a fun class to complement core requirements.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Respected--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Dr. Langer is personable, knowledgeable, and engaging. While a lot of his material is more experiential and anecdotal than strict reading, it comes from a career of success in a variety of fields. He can be a bit rigid on certain concepts, but his class is energetic and enjoyable from start to finish.

Hard to believe it's been almost 10 years since I had him at Columbia. He was the face of the Technology Management program. I almost needed a chiropractor carrying around the 6 or so books he required but other than that I highly recommend him. I found him to be engaging, enthusiastic and knowledgeable. He poured his heart into the program.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment: Art was joke, no one took his class seriously. He was offensive to students and unprepared for class. Not an effective instructor, unhelpful feedback and unclear course assignments. Knowledgeable as if frequently watches shark tank.
Excellent professor.  Demanding but fair.

Langer is a visionary, who is the "godfather" of the Columbia University Technology Management program.  He teaches the 1st executive seminar, product realization, and the introductory Technology in a Business Environment.  He prepares middle management bound IT to succeed with and become senior execs, many of whom mentor in the program.
He is a good guy.

Professor Armstrong is the greatest professor in this world. Taking Professor Armstrong's class was one of the greatest choices in my life. He is really friendly, and I've learned so much from his class, and I'd love to take his class again in the future. Take his class, you will love the professor and the great lectures.

The Best Professor in this World. You Must Take His Class. I truely respect Professor Armstrong and his class due to the fact that he is the most prestigious professor in this field. Taking his class was one of the best choices in my life. Thank you Professor Armstrong for the friendliness!!^^

One of the toughest professors at Columbia. To get an A from his course, a ton of efforts would be required. Very profound readings difficult to understand even for Koreans. Never ever recommend his every course :(
The readings in this Vietnam War seminar were outstanding.  The professor is extremely inconsistent with grading and he offers little valuable feedback until way after the middle of the term.
No Comments
No Comments
He's a good teacher and the materials are not difficult. The grades are reasonable. But the time of the class is too late and I'm already tired with other classes.

In all my years in college, I can safely say that Mehdi was the worst instructor I ever had. Lectures should be attended once, if only to experience something so shockingly bad you can only laugh in disbelief. After that, prepare to crack open the book and hit up google because you are on your own. Do not take unless absolutely necessary.
He is knowledgeable with a lot of practical past experiences.
His lectures are legen ... wait for it ... dary

As explained by Professor Stinson the course "Code#" refers to the Three Days Rule (Which was created by Jesus, BTDubbs) and well... You know! ;) Stinson is a truly inspirational prof. He really cares about his students and will go the extra mile to help them succeed. "Sometimes we search for one thing but discover another." - Barney Stinson

The Barnman is fantastic!  I took his course, Awesomeness 101, and he is an expert in the field!  He uses two textbooks, the Bro Code and the Playbook.  These are probably the only books I WANT TO READ outside of class!  If you want someone to "teach you how to live", he's the guy.  He is straight up legen- wait for it- DARY!

Barbara is an amazing professor: caring, understanding, and simply amazing! Her discussions are always insightful and thought-provoking. She gives great feedback for your work and is always accessible outside of class. While participation somewhat matters, you won't regret taking any of her classes.
What a sweet and caring human being. She is very compassionate and considerate of others.

I took her for my internship seminar at City College. Leave it to a Columbia Professor to let me have any great memories of CCNY. Unlike any other professors at CCNY, Professor Simon was very thoughtful and caring towards her students. You read great short stories and discuss them. Will miss this class.

Best professor in the school. Take a class with her if you get the chance. She is brilliant. All of her lectures are straight from her head. She has every historical date memorized from the past 400 years. So warm and empathetic. So helpful. I loved her so much that I made sure I took more classes with her just because she was the professor.
She is the BEST professor I've had at CUSSW. Take a class with her.

I was really disappointed with this class. All we did was watch movies every week and then talk about them. We had to buy 4 brand new textbooks (costing over $200) and we rarely discussed them in class.
Excellent professor, a luminary, clone her!!!

Class Advocacy! irrelevant class readings. no lecture at all. class consisted of only boring discussions. felt like a clinical class. do not take it! i have heard she is a bit better in other subjects, but this one was terrible

If you have the opportunity to take a class, ANY class, with Barbara Simon, DO NOT PASS IT UP!! She is probably one of the two or three best teachers at the school-- a feeling shared unanimously by all. She is also a tremendous human being, brilliant and wise. When you are with her, you feel like you are in the presence of greatness.
Great class, but no direction. Learn more grammar than ever before.
Outstanding Professor!!
Outstanding Instructor
Outstanding Professor; he should be Professor of the Year
Doc's class is not only the entertaining but make you really want to learn the material.  I never thought I would enjoy Statistics as much as  I did!
Dr Batteh is an amazing Professor.   I highly recommend taking his class.
Outstanding Professor
Outstanding at making the concepts easy to comprehend. one of the best classes I have taken.
Dr. Batteh is fantastic!  One of the most entertaining Instructors I have ever had!
dr. batteh was my favorite professor.  his class is fast paced and entertaining.  I recommend his class to anyone who is prepared to work.. otherwise don't
TOUGH AS HELL!  Doc definitely makes the subject matter interesting however his exams are a B.....
Dr. Batteh was fantastic... LOVED the class and his energy!
TOUGH TOUGH TOUGH
Dr Batteh was an amazing Instructor.  He made the subject matter less complex and very easy to understand.  I highly recommend taking his class.

Araujo made my first semester at an Ivy League seem bearable. In a class of students of 200 plus, she managed to be personable and caring. There  were always long lines to speak with her. She's instantly likable and has a different approach to teaching. She engages the class, makes you critically think and laugh out loud while doing it. Love her!!!
Dr. Araujo is an amazing human being. She teaches by example and has an amazing approach to help you problem solve in any situation. The best professor at Columbia- hands and stethoscopes down!!!
Excellent professor, skilled at engaging students in meaningful discussion, enriches discussion with well-founded insight and an impressive commitment to teaching.
Be warned. Ask those who have had her for courses and integration liaison for opinion and advice. Keep very quiet and do your best to fly under the radar.
She is not nice.

Dr. Araujo is intelligent regarding the course material and knows exactly how to communicate the information in a way that is both informative and understandable to students. She is an extremely effective lecturer who is able to hold the attention of her students. I am so impressed with her knowledge and lecturing style. What an amazing professor!

Really just an amazing professor! She understands how to put things into perspective. Bmrilliantly funny, empathetic, and oh so interesting. Araujo exemplifies the quintessential educator. Everyone loves her class!!!!
Great class. Well organized. Strong advocate for women. Understanding when issues come up, she's willing to help you with test taking tips.

This professor has her teaching down to a science. The lectures are organized with just the right amount of info on her slides. Class discussions are fun and open. The entire clss environment was delightful. I met with this professor several mornings BEFORE class and she was nothing but accomadating. She's great at putting things into perspective.

This professor is awesome!!!  So full of life and information. Not only does she help you with anything possible, but you can go to her for study tips, career advise or anything else you need help with because she is that dedicated to her students. She always went above and beyond with the students. LOVED HER, HER NOTES, AND HER ORGANIZED CLASS1111

In only five short weeks, Professor Araujo taught our class all about OB, Women's Health and Newborns!! She frequently will repeat important information through out the lecture and even did a "game show quiz-like" review to make us comfortable with the material. Her lectures are very organized. I also like that she asked us about our clinical exp.

Araujo is the best Professor ever!!! We laughed in this class and had a great time...while we were learning. She doesn't try to trick you or act like she is above you. Down to earth, approachable, and clearly an expert in the field. This class didn't last long enough for me. I loved it! I also had Prof Araujo for clinical...I lucked out!!!

araujo was horrible, obnoxious & unreasonable; We will make sure all the prospective students avoid her, she is mean, rude, has no patience, has no recollection how she struggled in school to get where she is; her teaching skills are dreadful!!!

I really enjoyed this class and this professor. You actually pay attention in this class because you are not only learning, but being entertained. Lots of tricks to help us remember everything. Araujo is one of my favorite Profs of all time!

Excellent teacher. If you pay attention in her class you will learn. Some students complained because she actually requires tht you READ the textbook, but the text in this class was well worth the money. Very dedicated and interesting to watch during lecture. I was never bored in her class, but rather very interested in what was going to be said ne

As a guy in a mostly all girl class about OB and women's health, I was surprised how Professor Araujo kept me interested in every topic. She is funny and enthusiastic, which even allowed me to enjoy the 6 hour marathon Mondays. We had a few obnoxious students in our class, but they were no match for Professor Araujo's quick wit. Learned a lot!

I was having a tough time and Professor Araujo really helped me out a lot. I met with her several times for one on one "extra help" sessions. She is very dedicated and one of the best Professors Columbia has to offer. Strict, but you will know your stuff when you leave her class. I'm sad her class is over
FUN CLASS! I almost forgot I was learning. Very dedicated, loves students. Keeps the wise guys in check. Breaks every hour are a plus!

Thought I would hate this class, but she made me love it. She is very interesting and shares her personal/professional experiences related to the class. Dr.Araujo has total control of the class and she doesn't let the attention seeking question-happy students take over the class. Helps students learn instead of remember. One of the best teachers!
One word. Awesome!!!! Too bad that all professors are not as dedicated as she is.

While I am not that interested in this topic, (OB)I must say that she made it  interesting. Professor Araujo is always available to students and frequently answers her e-mails within a few hours of receiving them. Exams are always graded returned within a few hours. She's funny and a great prof. Wish all of my undergrad. prof were this great!!!

Wow!Dr. Araujo is unreal! She clearly is an expert in Women's Health/OB/Newborn, etc. Her class is interesting and fast paced, but yet you never feel rushed. Her lectures are organized and chock full of info. She has a way to help us remember everything! I realized that I am, for the first time, not memorizing information, but rather processing it!
Prof. Araujo is awesome!! She teaches difficult topics with ease and simplicity. She has control over the class which is a nice change. Funny and approachable. She makes learning fun!!

I loved this professor. This was one of my first classes at Columbia University and I was rather intimidated, but Professor Araujo made all of us feel at ease. She is straight forward and fun to watch. She is entertaining and gives students helpful tricks to remember things. She makes even complicated topics seem easy.Araujo is awesome!!

I love Professor Araujo! She is smart, funny, and caring. She is open to helping any student at any time. She always makes time for her students, even if the concerns aren't about the class she is teaching. She is a great someone who I look up to!!

I love Professor Araujo! She is smart, funny, and caring. She keeps her classes in control and doesn't let side conversations (hate those) disturb the class. She is open to helping any student at any time. She always makes time for her students, even if the concerns aren't about the class she is teaching. She is a great someone who I look up to!!

Prof. Araujo is extremely animated which keeps the class interesting and fun. She has a trick to remembering everything and her philosophy is to work smarter not harder. She answers student e-mails with lightening speed and has made it a point to help students in any way possible. I learned more from her course than from any other class at Columbia
No Comments

Professor Araujo demands a lot from her students. This is not a class you can fall asleep in!! She moves about the class during discussions and does helpful demonstrations- she is very enthusiastic. She speaks at a pace that allows one to take notes and will repeat important points throughout her lectures. Professor Araujo encourages participation

I got so much out of this class. From the wide range of meditation exercises to the dynamic class discussions and in-class debate, this course was appropriately challenging, exceptionally thought-provoking, and always engaging. Excellent survey of the exciting field of Positive Psychology, presenting a wide range of cutting-edge research. Awesome!

Very informative, engaging, and thought provoking course! He was helpful in responding to the questions that everyone had during our class and responded well to emails. He was open to everyone's different interpretations of the articles and created a safe space to share your opinions. Great guided meditation series throughout the class!

Instructor held space very well in an inclusive fashion while also guiding and providing helpful feedback on subject matter. The instructor has both an in-depth and very sophisticated knowledge in the field but also exhibits passion for the material and topics. He is also very engaging, sincere and cares about his student's learning experience.

I learned so much about a new spectrum in clinical psychology. The theories were interesting, but the whole roundedness and his passion for this research area were inspirational. Experiencing mindfulness through each class session offer very new vibe to my TC courses. He is a greatteacher and a true advocate who believes in positivity in our lives.

Professor Barton inspired me to create a daily habit of mindfulness and consider positive psychology as an important facet in a broad range of modalities and treatments. Yakov explained concepts very well. He created a class space that was structured while at the same time encouraging for students to freely express thoughts without judgement.

Great class, with very interesting discussions. The professor put a lot of effort into keeping it interesting and every session was value adding. Great group discussion and interesting assignments. The professor managed to create a great atmophere in the class. I would always take this class again.

Yakov is an amazing resource  within the positive psychology field.  His positive psychology class is uniquely positioned to showcase his extensive experience with the best mindfulness training techniques that combine leading psychology research with age-old meditation knowledge.

Positive Psychology was a class for which I had very high expectations, and Dr. Barton matched them all. I am so grateful to have learned so much about PosPsy, and the readings were almost universally great. His lectures are clear and he is very supportive inout of class. But he is a tougher grader than he appears, and his tests can be tricky.

I took Professor Barton's Positive Psychology class at Columbia University, Teachers College. Uses a fascinating approach to teaching positive psychology material, including experiential meditationrelaxation exercises, lectures, group activities, films, and a class debate on the current state of the field. Learned a ton, highly recommended!
One of the best professors I've had at Columbia. Professor Beck is very dedicated to helping his students.
Amazing teacher, one of the best at Columbia, in my opinion. Would definitely take his class agian!

Professor Beck is very knowledgeable about the subject matter. The class was very productive in terms of material covered, which seems rare at CSSW. Lots of dad jokes but a sweet and smart guy. Be prepared to participate (and read!)

Keren Bergman is inspirational
She will guide your path to discovery and perhaps a great career.
She motivates and inspires
A truly great lady and teacher
fantastic teacher that really knows how to bring stuff across - and yes - very hot.
Great teacher - knows everything, very approachable - and hot!
She is a good teacher, with a nice mix of exercises and getting the concepts across.

Prof Bell was okay, take her if you want an easy A. Just wouldn't personally take her again because I felt like I didn't learn anything; lectures were mainly tangents and all over the place. Don't take her for a crucial course

Joan Bell is kind but doesn't respond to emails and does not get through the content. She would miss lectures, or spend all classes on tangents and not even open the powerpoint. I really wanted more clinical education and this class barely even scraped the surface of the few topics we were supposed to cover.

Joan is a radiant professor. Her lectures are so engaging and interactive. Such a friendly and fun person, so perfect to learn about social work with children with. Response papers are easy and straightforward, and final was very reasonable!
I took this class as an elective on a whim and ended up thoroughly enjoying it due to Prof. Bell's wonderful teaching style! I would definitely recommend taking a class with her.

Bhaven is really inspiring professor. He is clear about what kind of topic are useful for an student and address the class in these direction. Also, he is very involved in the field, knowing and working with the lead authors, contextualizing several papers with his personal history and perspective. Really funny moments and good in feedback
Awesome prof.  Funniest guy ever!

Prof. Sampat is phenomenal.  He's a great discussion leader, he's engaging, and he's playful.  The class is rich but the pace is not overwhelming.  I learned lots about policy issues in pharmaceutical development and distribution.
Very challenging course.

Although she was not an easy grader, and her lectures were at times a tad boring (could've been very well the subject I was bored about), I'd recommend taking her class if you like to challenge yourself. She is very nice and seems fair with grading.

She is an experienced practitioner who is grounded in old-school social work values and background. As such, she at times utilizes problematic and somewhat offensive ways of teaching subject such as the Civil Rights era. Pointless and unproductive daily quizzes. This is someone who is not a good teacher, but better at research.

I think I'm most struck by, and appreciative of, Dr. Blair's thoughtfulness as a coach. I think anyone working with her can anticipate receiving attention that reflects your experience, your needs, her experience, and her desire to see you thrive. This does not mean she is always easy, but will both push and protect you as needed.
Madelyn is very aligned with the learning objectives of our course. Her mentoring and coaching were tailored to what we needed.

Responsive to questions within a reasonable timeframe.  Offers constructive experience-based guidance intended to aid the student and increase individual learning.  Lectures are interesting, thought-stimulating and practical; a mix of presentation and application of concepts via mini-breakouts.  Applies story-telling as an effective learning aid.

Dr. Blair has provided thought-provoking in-person lectures on storytelling and resilience. She coached me on ways I can minimize stress before presenting and focus on my message. The end result was a successful presentation because of her coaching! I would jump at the opportunity to attend any course where Dr. Madelyn Blair teaches!

I love Dr. Blairs powerful message about three leadership qualities: insatiable curiosity, self aware and deep knowledge. She has helped me become a better knowledge professional and her work has been eye opening. Anyone would be lucky to have the opportunity to work with someone who is as passionate and wise as Dr. Blair. She is simply amazing!

Dr. Blair is a fantastic instructor. She excels at effectively communicating the course content, including breaking down and simplifying concepts. Additionally, she really cares about her students. I asked for a one on one meeting to discuss the course and my progress within the course, and she was very receptive, providing constructive feedback an
Blum is an impassioned teacher with high standards. I learned a lot. Very funny, too.
Horrible class, completely inarticulate, and a joke to the j-school.
Blum is amazing. He really knows how to inspire you with his honesty. Very funny, too. Younger and cooler than most of the j-school profs.
Blum is funny and laid back, but he'll make you think and he'll give you as much time as you need.  He'll improve your writing -- and your confidence in yourself.
No Comments
No Comments
No Comments

Professor Shapiro is a rare thing at Columbia and among professors in general these days - he genuinely cares about his students and takes the time to assist them in any way he can. And he looks almost exactly like Woody Allen, which was extra fun for me as someone who previously lived in the Midwest and imagined New Yorkers to be just like Allen.
SUPERB TEACHER! Truly cares deeply about his students' progress.
No Comments

Bob is an incredibly caring person who will work tirelessly to help his students. As they should, his classes are challenging. You'll learn more than you ever expected. Do yourself a favor ... take at least one with him before you graduate.
Class is very proof heavy but every proof is supported by a motivating example. I was struggling with the math but office hours are very helpful to learn some tricks and make it a little easier
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Good interactive teaching style where the students get the opportunity to think about a problem before the professor reveals the path to the solution. A strong algebraic background is essential or it will be very hard to follow along.
Professor Brandl ran a great seminar that helped me to deepen my understanding of electricity and magnetism. While the content is rigorous, he manages to bring the message across.
reviews.rating_tags: Respected--Skip class? You won't pass.--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Super nice and she really cares about her students. Her lectures are great and she provides so much practice for her midterms and tests. She is great.

I thought that she humanized chemistry in a unique and Canadian way.  She brought discussion into the lecture and used back-and-forth creatively.  The tests were hard but fair, and she knew her stuff.

She was a horrible teacher... that is probably one of the reasons she was fired from Columbia University.  She just couldn't hack it at such a high level institution.  And (I know this is just an opinion) she was really weird.  She had a frantic and defensive attitude about her which made her seem like a psycho.  Thank GOD she is gone.
No Comments
No Comments
No Comments

By any means necessary, AVOID Thomas Brambor. An absolute unpleasure to have encountered and would single this out as the single biggest regret in uni. Unhelpful and willfully distant and disconnected from his students. Refuses to work with students to become successful, outsources all his responsibilities as a lecturer and professor to his TA's.

If you take this class, you will learn a ton of useful tools. However, don't expect the professor to teach you. All the good stuff you wilteach yourself to solv the tough assignments. Homework assignemnts are vague and poorly communicated. the harder questions will have you googling for hours. It's very hands off. Too hands off. I dread this class.

My most disliked class so far at QMSS. 1. professor uses weird math heavy examples that have no translation to the rest of the class assignments 2. homework assignments are so vague and the ta's all respond with (as long as you display you understand the material that's all we care about) and then are the stingiest graders. I could go on. Hate it.
